parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Pass value for combination of flags you need. Add 1 to get flat list instead of fields and children as items within the parent field. Add 2 to include font information for field and edit fonts. Add 4 to include font buffer data.

examples.label: Query JSON:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$JSON
; Value:
MBS
( "DynaPDF.GetFieldsAsJSON"; PDF ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.GetFieldsAsJSON"; PDF ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$JSON</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Query document information:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$pdf
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.New
") ]
# Load PDF from container
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer
";
$pdf
;
PDF JSON::PDF
) ]
# import a page
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile
";
$pdf
; 1) ]
#
# query all fields:
Set Variable [
$JSON
; Value:
MBS
("DynaPDF.GetFieldsAsJSON";
$pdf
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
Set Variable [
$JSON
; Value:
MBS
("
JSON.Colorize
";
$JSON
) ]
End If
#
# store in field
Set Field [
PDF JSON::JSON
;
$JSON
]
# Release memory
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.Release
";
$pdf
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.New") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pdf</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Load PDF from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; $pdf; PDF JSON::PDF) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> import a page</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile"; $pdf; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> query all fields:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.GetFieldsAsJSON"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$JSON</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") = 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.Colorize"; $JSON) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$JSON</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> store in field</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$JSON </Calculation>
    <Field table="PDF JSON" id="99999" name="JSON "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Release memory</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.Release"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DynaPDF.GetFontAsJSON
functions.description: Queries one font within the PDF as JSON object. See TPDFFontInfo structure in DynaPDF help, which we convert to JSON for you. Keys include Ascent, AvgWidth, BaseEncoding, BaseFont, CapHeight, CharSet, CharSetSize, CIDOrdering, CIDRegistry, CIDSet, CIDSetSize, CIDSupplement, CIDToGIDMap, CIDToGIDMapSize, CMapBuf, CMapBufSize, CMapName, Descent, Encoding, FirstChar, Flags, FontBBox, FontBuffer, FontBufSize, FontFamily, FontFilePath, FontFileType, FontName, FontStretch, FontType, FontWeight, FullName, HaveEncoding, HorzWidths, HorzWidthsCount, Imported, ItalicAngle, Lang, LastChar, Leading, Length1, Length2, Length3, MaxWidth, Metadata, MetadataSize, MisWidth, Panose, PostScriptName, SpaceWidth, StemH, StemV, ToUnicode, ToUnicodeSize, VertDefPos, VertWidths, VertWidthsCount, WMode, XHeight and Index. See also GetFontAsJSON function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.GetActionsAsJSON DynaPDF.GetDocInfoAsJSON DynaPDF.GetFieldAsJSON DynaPDF.GetFieldsAsJSON DynaPDF.GetFontCount DynaPDF.GetFontOrigin DynaPDF.GetFontsAsJSON DynaPDF.GetFontWeight DynaPDF.New Release notes Version 10.5 Added DynaPDF.GetAnnotationAsJSON , DynaPDF.GetColorSpaceAsJSON , DynaPDF.GetEmbeddedFileAsJSON , DynaPDF.GetFieldAsJSON and DynaPDF.GetFontAsJSON functions. Blog Entries New in MBS FileMaker Plugin 10.5 MBS FileMaker Plugin, version 10.5pr1 Embrace JSON FileMaker Magazin Ausgabe 6/2020, Seite 26 This function checks for a license. Created 19th September 2020 , last changed 2nd September 2022 DynaPDF.GetFieldsAsJSON - DynaPDF.GetFontCount 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: DynaPDF.GetFontOrigin
functions.description: Returns the current font origin. The font origin is automatically set to orTopLeft or orDownLeft if the coordinate system will be changed. The origin can be changed at runtime with the function DynaPDF.SetFontOrigin . Returns DownLeft (BaseLine) or TopLeft (Upper left corner of the font's bounding box). See also GetFontOrigin function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.GetFontAsJSON DynaPDF.GetFontSize DynaPDF.GetFontWeight DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.SetFont DynaPDF.SetFontOrigin DynaPDF.SetFontWeight This function checks for a license. Created 21st October 2014 , last changed 21st October 2014 DynaPDF.GetFontCount - DynaPDF.GetFontSelMode 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: DynaPDF.GetFontSelMode
functions.description: Returns the current font selection mode. The font selection mode describes what kind of font name must be passed to DynaPDF.SetFont . Note that a font cannot be found if the wrong mode is set. See also GetFontSelMode function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.GetFontSize DynaPDF.GetFontWeight DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.SetFont DynaPDF.SetFontSelMode This function checks for a license. functions.name: DynaPDF.GetFontsAsJSON
functions.description:
Queries all fonts within the PDF as JSON array. See TPDFFontInfo structure in DynaPDF help, which we convert to JSON for you. Keys include Ascent, AvgWidth, BaseEncoding, BaseFont, CapHeight, CharSet, CharSetSize, CIDOrdering, CIDRegistry, CIDSet, CIDSetSize, CIDSupplement, CIDToGIDMap, CIDToGIDMapSize, CMapBuf, CMapBufSize, CMapName, Descent, Encoding, FirstChar, Flags, FontBBox, FontBuffer, FontBufSize, FontFamily, FontFilePath, FontFileType, FontName, FontStretch, FontType, FontWeight, FullName, HaveEncoding, HorzWidths, HorzWidthsCount, Imported, ItalicAngle, Lang, LastChar, Leading, Length1, Length2, Length3, MaxWidth, Metadata, MetadataSize, MisWidth, Panose, PostScriptName, SpaceWidth, StemH, StemV, ToUnicode, ToUnicodeSize, VertDefPos, VertWidths, VertWidthsCount, WMode, XHeight and Index. See also GetFontsAsJSON function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Query JSON: Copy Text Copy XML Set Variable [ $JSON ; Value: MBS ( "DynaPDF.GetFontsAsJSON"; PDF ) ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.GetFontsAsJSON"; PDF ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$JSON</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Query fonts: Copy Text Copy XML Set Variable [ $pdf ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.New ") ] # Load PDF from container Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer "; $pdf ; PDF JSON::PDF ) ] # import a page Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile "; $pdf ; 1) ] # # query all fonts: Set Variable [ $JSON ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.GetFieldsAsJSON "; $pdf ) ] If [ MBS ("IsError") = 0 ] Set Variable [ $JSON ; Value: MBS (" JSON.Colorize "; $JSON ) ] End If # # store in field Set Field [ PDF JSON::JSON ; $JSON ] # Release memory Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Release "; $pdf ) ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

functions.name: DynaPDF.GetFullyQualifiedFieldName
functions.description: Returns the fully qualified name of a field. Note that children of a radio button, for example, have no name by its own. The export value must be compared to distinguish the children fields in this case. See also GetFullyQualifiedFieldName function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.New Release notes Version 14.0 Added DynaPDF.GetFullyQualifiedFieldName function. Blog Entries MBS FileMaker Plugin, version 13.6pr1 This function checks for a license. functions.name: DynaPDF.GetGlyphIndex
functions.description:
Returns the glyph index of the given code page or Unicode index. How Index must be defined depends on the code page with which the font was loaded. For example, if the font was loaded with a 8 bit code page, then indexes below 256 are treated as index into the code page table. Higher indexes are treated as Unicode value in this case. Note that all Unicode encoded glyphs of the font are accessible in this case, also if the font was loaded with a 8 bit code page! At time of publication the function does not support CJK code pages which require a conversion to Unicode, e.g. cpCJK_Big5_Uni, cpCJK_EUC_JP_Uni, and so on. See also GetGlyphIndex function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Queries glyph indexes for two letters: Copy Text Copy XML MBS ( "DynaPDF.GetGlyphIndex"; $PDF ; Code("A") ) & ¶ & MBS ( "DynaPDF.GetGlyphIndex"; $PDF ; Code("z") ) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.GetGlyphIndex"; $PDF; Code("A") ) & ¶ & MBS( "DynaPDF.GetGlyphIndex"; $PDF; Code("z") )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> See also DynaPDF.GetFieldIndex DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.SetFieldIndex Release notes Version 14.5 Added DynaPDF.GetGlyphIndex and DynaPDF.GetGlyphOutline functions. Blog Entries New in MBS FileMaker Plugin in Version 14.5 MBS FileMaker Plugin, version 14.5pr1 This function checks for a license. Created 26th September 2024 , last changed 26th September 2024 DynaPDF.GetGStateFlags - DynaPDF.GetGlyphOutline 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: DynaPDF.GetImage
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.GetImage"; PDF; Index; Selector { ; FileName; ImageFormat } )
functions.description: Queries image properties or content. Retrieves the properties of an image as well as the decompressed image buffer if needed. By default all images are returned decompressed, with exception of image types which are already stored in a valid file format like JPEG and JPEG 2000 images. If all image types should be decompressed set the flag pfDecompressAllImages. Selectors include:

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the image from 0 to DynaPDF.GetImageCount -1.

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: Which value to query.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: In case we return a container value, the file name to use.

parameters.name: ImageFormat
parameters.description: Which image file format to use for the image returned. Default is to use JPEG for JPEG images and TIFF for other formats. Value can be TIFF, JPEG, PNG, BMP or JPC.

    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="6" name="Go to Layout">

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.New") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pdf</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Load PDF from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; $pdf; Start::SourcePDF) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Put result in records</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="6" name="Go to Layout">
    <LayoutDestination value="SelectedLayout"/>
    <Layout id="99999" name="List"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="10" name="Delete All Records">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.GetImageCount"; $PDF ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$imageCount</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$imageIndex</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $imageCount > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$imageIndex </Calculation>
    <Field table="List" id="99999" name="Index "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.GetImage"; $pdf; $ImageIndex; "Picture"; ""; "") </Calculation>
    <Field table="List" id="99999" name="Image "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.GetImage"; $pdf; $ImageIndex; "Picture"; "test.jpg"; "JPEG") </Calculation>
    <Field table="List" id="99999" name="JPEG "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.GetImage"; $pdf; $ImageIndex; "Picture"; "test.bmp"; "BMP") </Calculation>
    <Field table="List" id="99999" name="BMP "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.GetImage"; $pdf; $ImageIndex; "Picture"; "test.png"; "PNG") </Calculation>
    <Field table="List" id="99999" name="PNG "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.GetImage"; $pdf; $ImageIndex; "Picture"; "test.tif"; "TIFF") </Calculation>
    <Field table="List" id="99999" name="TIFF "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="False"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$imageIndex + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$imageIndex</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $imageIndex >= $imageCount </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.Release"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DynaPDF.GetImageContainerCount
functions.description: Queries number of images in an image container. The function determines the number of images in a multi-page image in the same way as DynaPDF.GetImageFileCount , but accepts a container value as input. At time of publication TIFF images are supported only. See also GetImageContainerCount function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.GetImage DynaPDF.GetImageFileCount DynaPDF.New Blog Entries Top 10 from the MBS Plugin in 2022 MBS FileMaker Plugin 12.4 This function checks for a license. Created 2nd September 2022 , last changed 2nd September 2022 DynaPDF.GetImage - DynaPDF.GetImageCount 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

functions.name: DynaPDF.GetImageCount
functions.description:
Queries number of images in PDF. Returns the number of image objects which were loaded with DynaPDF functions or imported from external PDF files. This does not include inline images. The images can be accessed with DynaPDF.GetImage . If you need to write all files to disk, you can use DynaPDF.ExtractImages or use DynaPDF.ListImages to list them all with details. See also GetImageCount function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Loop and get all images in various formats: Copy Text Copy XML Go to Layout [ “Start” (Start) ; Animation: None ] Set Variable [ $pdf ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.New ") ] # Load PDF from container Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer "; $pdf ; Start::SourcePDF ) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile "; $pdf ) ] # Put result in records Go to Layout [ “List” (List) ; Animation: None ] Delete All Records [ With dialog: Off ] Set Variable [ $imageCount ; Value: MBS ( "DynaPDF.GetImageCount"; $PDF ) ] Set Variable [ $imageIndex ; Value: 0 ] If [ $imageCount > 0 ] Loop New Record/Request Set Field [ List::Index ; $imageIndex ] Set Field [ List::Image ; MBS (" DynaPDF.GetImage "; $pdf ; $ImageIndex ; "Picture"; ""; "") ] Set Field [ List::JPEG ; MBS (" DynaPDF.GetImage "; $pdf ; $ImageIndex ; "Picture"; "test.jpg"; "JPEG") ] Set Field [ List::BMP ; MBS (" DynaPDF.GetImage "; $pdf ; $ImageIndex ; "Picture"; "test.bmp"; "BMP") ] Set Field [ List::PNG ; MBS (" DynaPDF.GetImage "; $pdf ; $ImageIndex ; "Picture"; "test.png"; "PNG") ] Set Field [ List::TIFF ; MBS (" DynaPDF.GetImage "; $pdf ; $ImageIndex ; "Picture"; "test.tif"; "TIFF") ] Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: On ] Set Variable [ $imageIndex ; Value: $imageIndex + 1 ] Exit Loop If [ $imageIndex >= $imageCount ] End Loop End If Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Release "; $pdf ) ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.GetImage"; $pdf; $ImageIndex; "Picture"; "test.tif"; "TIFF") </Calculation>
    <Field table="List" id="99999" name="TIFF "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="False"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>

    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$imageIndex</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">

  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>

</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DynaPDF.GetImportDocInfoAsJSON
functions.description:
Queries all document information from import file as JSON array. JSON includes text for various document information properties including Author, Creator, Keywords, Producer, Subject, Title, Company, PDF-X Version, PDF-X Conf, CreationDate and ModDate. As custom keys can be defined in PDF, those may get included, too. The DynaPDF.GetImportDocInfoAsJSON function is to read directly from open import PDF without importing it. Once imported, you can use DynaPDF.GetDocInfoAsJSON to get what is in the working PDF in memory. See also GetImportDocInfoAsJSON function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Query JSON: Copy Text Copy XML Set Variable [ $JSON ; Value: MBS ( "DynaPDF.GetImportDocInfoAsJSON"; PDF ) ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.GetImportDocInfoAsJSON"; PDF ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$JSON</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> See also DynaPDF.GetDocInfo DynaPDF.GetDocInfoAsJSON DynaPDF.GetImportDocInfo DynaPDF.New Release notes Version 10.5 Added DynaPDF.GetAnnotationsAsJSON , DynaPDF.GetBookmarksAsJSON , DynaPDF.GetColorSpacesAsJSON , DynaPDF.GetDocInfoAsJSON , DynaPDF.GetEmbeddedFilesAsJSON , DynaPDF.GetFieldsAsJSON , DynaPDF.GetFontsAsJSON , DynaPDF.GetImportDocInfoAsJSON and DynaPDF.SysFontInfoAsJSON functions. Blog Entries Unlocking the Power of PDFs in FileMaker with DynaPDF and the MBS FileMaker Plugin Things you can do with DynaPDF New in MBS FileMaker Plugin 10.5 MBS FileMaker Plugin, version 10.5pr1 Embrace JSON FileMaker Magazin Ausgabe 5/2021, Seite 26 Ausgabe 6/2020, Seite 26 This function checks for a license. Created 16th September 2020 , last changed 10th April 2022 DynaPDF.GetImportDocInfo - DynaPDF.GetImportFlags 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: DynaPDF.GetImportOrientation
functions.description: Queries orientation for input page. The function returns the orientation of a specific page within the currently open import file. If the function succeeds the return value is the orientation of the page in degrees. Possible values are 0, 90, 180, 270, or the same values as negative numbers. If the function fails, it returns an error. See also GetImportOrientation function in DynaPDF manual. functions.name: DynaPDF.GetImportPageBounds
functions.description:
Queries page size for an import PDF page. Please note that we have DynaPDF.GetPageBBox for the current working PDF and the DynaPDF.GetImportPageBounds for the current open import PDF. See also GetImportPageBounds function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Queries crop size of page 5 from import PDF: Copy Text Copy XML $r = MBS ( "DynaPDF.GetImportPageBounds"; $PDF ; 5; "CropBox" ) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.GetImportPageBounds"; $PDF; 5; "CropBox" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Query width and height of first page in PDF: Copy Text Copy XML # Initialize DynaPDF if needed If [ MBS (" DynaPDF.IsInitialized ")  ≠  1 ] Perform Script [ Specified: From list ; “Initialize DynaPDF” ; Parameter:    ] End If # Clear current PDF document Set Variable [ $pdf ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.New ") ] # # Load PDF from container # Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer "; $pdf ; Test::PDF artwork) ] # # Query page size for the first page: # Set Variable [ $PageSize ; Value: MBS ("DynaPDF.GetImportPageBounds"; $pdf ; 1; "MediaBox") ] # # split numbers, may need special handling with Math.TextToNumber to make sure it works if you use comma as decimal separator: # Set Variable [ $left ; Value: MBS ( " Math.TextToNumber "; GetValue( $PageSize ; 1)) ] Set Variable [ $top ; Value: MBS ( " Math.TextToNumber "; GetValue( $PageSize ; 2)) ] Set Variable [ $right ; Value: MBS ( " Math.TextToNumber "; GetValue( $PageSize ; 3)) ] Set Variable [ $bottom ; Value: MBS ( " Math.TextToNumber "; GetValue( $PageSize ; 4)) ] # Set Variable [ $Width ; Value: Abs( $right - $left ) ] Set Variable [ $height ; Value: Abs( $top - $bottom ) ] # # Calculate in centimeters # Set Field [ Test::Job dimension horizontal ; Round( $Width * 2.54 / 72; 2) ] Set Field [ Test::Job dimension vertical ; Round( $Height * 2.54 / 72; 2) ] # # Cleanup Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Release "; $pdf ) ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Initialize DynaPDF if needed</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("DynaPDF.IsInitialized")  ≠  1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="1" name="Perform Script">
    <Calculation>  </Calculation>
    <Script id="99999" name="Initialize DynaPDF"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Clear current PDF document</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.New") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pdf</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Load PDF from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; $pdf; Test::PDF artwork) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Query page size for the first page:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.GetImportPageBounds"; $pdf; 1; "MediaBox") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$PageSize</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> split numbers, may need special handling with Math.TextToNumber to make sure it works if you use comma as decimal separator:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Math.TextToNumber"; GetValue($PageSize; 1)) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$left</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Math.TextToNumber"; GetValue($PageSize; 2)) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$top</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Math.TextToNumber"; GetValue($PageSize; 3)) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$right</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Math.TextToNumber"; GetValue($PageSize; 4)) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$bottom</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Abs($right - $left) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Width</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Abs($top- $bottom) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$height</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Calculate in centimeters</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>Round($Width * 2.54 / 72; 2) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Test" id="99999" name="Job dimension horizontal "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>Round($Height * 2.54 / 72; 2) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Test" id="99999" name="Job dimension vertical "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.Release"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> See also DynaPDF.GetImportPageCount DynaPDF.GetPageBBox DynaPDF.IsInitialized DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile DynaPDF.Release Math.TextToNumber Example Databases DynaPDF/Batch Preview DynaPDF/Convert to 2 Pages DynaPDF/Place Template Blog Entries Things you can do with DynaPDF Adding cutting lines for PDF in FileMaker Create two page PDF document in FileMaker Introducing DynaPDF to MBS Filemaker Plugin FileMaker Magazin Ausgabe 4/2022, Seite 33 Ausgabe 5/2021, Seite 26 Ausgabe 6/2016, Seite 24 This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 21st March 2021 DynaPDF.GetImportPDFVersion - DynaPDF.GetImportPageCount 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Clear current PDF document</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.New") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pdf</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Load PDF from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; $pdf; Test::PDF artwork) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Query page size for the first page:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.GetImportPageBounds"; $pdf; 1; "MediaBox") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$PageSize</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> split numbers, may need special handling with Math.TextToNumber to make sure it works if you use comma as decimal separator:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Math.TextToNumber"; GetValue($PageSize; 1)) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$left</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Math.TextToNumber"; GetValue($PageSize; 2)) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$top</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Math.TextToNumber"; GetValue($PageSize; 3)) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$right</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Math.TextToNumber"; GetValue($PageSize; 4)) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$bottom</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Abs($right - $left) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Width</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Abs($top- $bottom) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$height</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Calculate in centimeters</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>Round($Width * 2.54 / 72; 2) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Test" id="99999" name="Job dimension horizontal "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>Round($Height * 2.54 / 72; 2) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Test" id="99999" name="Job dimension vertical "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.Release"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DynaPDF.GetImportPageCount
functions.description:
Queries number of pages in the current opened import PDF. Please note that we have DynaPDF.GetPageCount for the current working PDF and the DynaPDF.GetImportPageCount for the current open import PDF. See also GetImportPageCount function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Load a PDF from container and query page count: Copy Text Copy XML $r = MBS (" DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer "; $PDF ; Test::data ) $pageCount = MBS ("DynaPDF.GetImportPageCount"; $PDF ) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; $PDF; Test::data)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.GetImportPageCount"; $PDF)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pageCount</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Import selected pages from one PDF and write into a new one: Copy Text Copy XML # New PDF Set Variable [ $pdf ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.New ")] # Open existing PDF file (or use DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer for container) Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile "; $pdf ; $path )] # Query page count Set Variable [ $pageCount ; Value: MBS ( "DynaPDF.GetImportPageCount"; $pdf )] # Import the pages you need Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage "; $pdf ; 1)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage "; $pdf ; 2)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage "; $pdf ; 3)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage "; $pdf ; $pagecount - 1)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage "; $pdf ; $pagecount - 2)] # Set output to this file path # MBS ( " DynaPDF.OpenOutputFile "; $pdf ; "/Users/cs/Desktop/output.pdf" ) # Write PDF Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Save "; $pdf )] # release memory Set Variable [ $r1 ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Release "; $pdf )] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> New PDF</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.New")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pdf</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Open existing PDF file (or use DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer for container)</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile"; $pdf; $path )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Query page count</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.GetImportPageCount"; $pdf )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pageCount</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Import the pages you need</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage"; $pdf; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage"; $pdf; 2)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage"; $pdf; 3)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage"; $pdf; $pagecount - 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage"; $pdf; $pagecount - 2)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Set output to this file path</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> MBS( "DynaPDF.OpenOutputFile"; $pdf; "/Users/cs/Desktop/output.pdf" )</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Write PDF</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.Save"; $pdf)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> release memory</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.Release"; $pdf)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r1</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> See also DynaPDF.GetColorSpaceCount DynaPDF.GetImageFileCount DynaPDF.GetImportOrientation DynaPDF.GetImportPageBounds DynaPDF.GetPageCount DynaPDF.OpenOutputFile DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile DynaPDF.Release DynaPDF.Save Example Databases DynaPDF/Apply Letter Paper to PDF DynaPDF/Batch Preview DynaPDF/Convert to 2 Pages DynaPDF/Generate Previews DynaPDF/Invoice/Invoice DynaPDF/Merge PDFs DynaPDF/PDF Differences DynaPDF/Shrink PDF pages DynaPDF/Split PDF DynaPDF/Tables Blog Entries Merge documents with DynaPDF Things you can do with DynaPDF Merge PDF with table of contents Create two page PDF document in FileMaker Normalize PDF orientation with MBS Plugins and DynaPDF Introducing DynaPDF to MBS Filemaker Plugin FileMaker Magazin Ausgabe 4/2022, Seite 33 Ausgabe 5/2021, Seite 26 Ausgabe 6/2016, Seite 24 This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 4th August 2016 DynaPDF.GetImportPageBounds - DynaPDF.GetInDocInfoCount 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: DynaPDF.GetInDocInfoCount
functions.description: Queries number of document info in import file. Returns the number of available document info entries of the currently opened import file or a negative error code on failure. The document info entries can be enumerated with DynaPDF.GetInDocInfoEx. See also GetInDocInfoCount function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.GetDocInfoCount DynaPDF.New Blog Entries Things you can do with DynaPDF FileMaker Magazin Ausgabe 5/2021, Seite 26 This function checks for a license. Created 6th February 2015 , last changed 6th February 2015 DynaPDF.GetImportPageCount - DynaPDF.GetInEncryptionInfo 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

functions.name: DynaPDF.GetInEncryptionInfo
functions.description: Retrieves encryption settings of the currently active import file. It is not meaningful to call this function on an unencrypted file. Therefore, call GetInIsEncrypted() beforehand to determine whether the file is encrypted.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

functions.name: DynaPDF.GetInFieldCount
functions.description: Returns the number of top level fields included in the currently opened import file. The number of top level fields may not match the number of fields in the PDF file. For example, interactive forms created by Adobe's Designer contain usually one top level group field that contains all other fields of the document. The return value would be 1 in this case because the children of group fields are not taken into account. See also GetInFieldCount function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.GetActionCount DynaPDF.GetAnnotCount DynaPDF.GetFieldColor DynaPDF.GetImageCount DynaPDF.GetImageFileCount DynaPDF.GetPageFieldCount DynaPDF.GetTemplCount DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.SetFieldColor DynaPDF.SetFieldFont This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 DynaPDF.GetInEncryptionInfo - DynaPDF.GetInIsCollection 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

functions.result: Returns the number of fields or error.

functions.name: DynaPDF.GetInIsCollection
functions.description: Whether the currently opened import file is a portable collection. The PDF file must be opened with DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer or DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile before this function can be called. See also GetInIsCollection function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile This function checks for a license. The PDF file must be opened with DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer or DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile before this function can be called. See also GetInIsEncrypted function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.GetInIsTrapped DynaPDF.GetUserRights DynaPDF.ImportEncryptionSettings DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile DynaPDF.TestPassword This function checks for a license. functions.description: Whether the currently open import file is a Tagged PDF file. The PDF file must be opened beforehand with DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer or DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile . The return value is a set flags indicating the tagging status of the file.

parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

functions.name: DynaPDF.GetInIsTrapped
functions.description: Queries value of the Trapped key in the current open import file. The PDF file must be opened with DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer or DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile beforehand. If the key is not set the return value is false. See also GetInIsTrapped function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.GetInIsEncrypted DynaPDF.GetInIsSigned DynaPDF.GetInIsTaggedPDF DynaPDF.GetTrapped DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile DynaPDF.SetTrapped This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 DynaPDF.GetInIsTaggedPDF - DynaPDF.GetInIsXFAForm 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

functions.name: DynaPDF.GetInIsXFAForm
functions.description: Queries whether the current open import file contains an XFA form. DynaPDF does not allow editing of XFA forms. If the form is a hybrid form the XFA part can be deleted and the remaining PDF form fields can be edited as usual. The PDF file must be opened with DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer or DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile before this function can be called. DynaPDF.GetXFAStream can be used to read the form definition and values. See also GetInIsXFAForm function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.DeleteXFAForm DynaPDF.GetXFAStream DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile Example Databases DynaPDF/Show PDF Fields This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 21st April 2018 DynaPDF.GetInIsTrapped - DynaPDF.GetInMetadata 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

functions.name: DynaPDF.GetInMetadata
functions.description:
Queries input PDF metadata. The function can be used to access the optional metadata streams of pages or the global metadata stream of the current open import file. Metadata streams are in XMP format that is a superset of XML. The PDF file must be opened with DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile or DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer beforehand. Although the global XMP stream does usually exist in todays PDF files, metadata streams are optional and maybe not present. The function empty text, if no metadata stream is present. See also GetInMetadata function in DynaPDF manual. functions.name: DynaPDF.GetInPrintSettings
functions.description: Retrieves the print settings of the current open import file. The PDF file must be opened with DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer or DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile before the function can be used. The print settings are used to initialize the print dialog in a viewer application. NumCopies, PickTrayByPDFSize and PrintRangesCount return a number (-1 if undefined). PrintRanges returns a list of values for the ranges. DuplexMode returns values like FlipShortEdge, FlipLongEdge, Simplex or None. PrintScaling returns None or AppDefault. See also DynaPDF.GetPrintSettings and DynaPDF.SetPrintSettings . See also GetInPrintSettings function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.GetPrintSettings DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile DynaPDF.SetPrintSettings This function checks for a license. Created 14th March 2015 , last changed 24th June 2024 DynaPDF.GetInPDFVersionEx - DynaPDF.GetInRepairMode 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: Which value to retrieve. Can be NumCopies, PickTrayByPDFSize, PrintRangesCount, PrintScaling, DuplexMode and PrintRanges.

functions.name: DynaPDF.GetInRepairMode
functions.description: Queries whether the current open import file was opened in repair or normal mode. When a PDF file was loaded in normal mode and if it contains damages which were not recognized when opening the file, e.g. if it is not possible to load one or more pages of it, then it is possible to load the file explicitly in repair mode to repair the damages. Not all damages can be repaired but DynaPDF contains a very robust and powerful PDF parser that is able to repair many damages in a PDF file. See also DynaPDF.OpenImportFile. If the current open import file was already opened in repair mode then the return value is 1. If it was loaded in normal mode then the return value is 0. If no import file is in memory the return value is a negative error code. See also GetInRepairMode function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.New This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 10th February 2025 DynaPDF.GetInPrintSettings - DynaPDF.GetIsFixedPitch 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

functions.name: DynaPDF.GetIsFixedPitch
functions.description: Queries whether the active font is a fixed pitch font or 0 if the font is a variable pitch font. You must set a font before this function can be used (see DynaPDF.SetFont for further information). See also GetIsFixedPitch function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.SetFont This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 DynaPDF.GetInRepairMode - DynaPDF.GetIsTaggingEnabled 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

functions.name: DynaPDF.GetIsTaggingEnabled
functions.description: Queries whether tagging is enabled. If the function is called within an open page then it checks whether tagging is enabled for this page. DynaPDF.EditPage does not fully disable tagging when the structure information of a specific page is damaged. When the function is called outside of an open page then it checks whether tagging for the entire document is enabled. Tagging can be disabled when a page of a PDF file with tagging information was imported while a structure tree was already in memory. See also GetIsTaggingEnabled function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.EditPage DynaPDF.New This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 DynaPDF.GetIsFixedPitch - DynaPDF.GetJPEGQuality 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

functions.result: Returns 1 if tagging is enabled, 0 if not or error.

functions.name: DynaPDF.GetJPEGQuality
functions.description: Queries the JPEG compression quality. The function returns the current JPEG compression quality which is used when compressing images with the JPEG or JPEG2000 compression filter. The return value can be negative indicating that the pass-through mode is disabled. The absolute value represents the whished image quality in percent if the JPEG compression filter is used. If the JPEG2000 compression filter is used the value represents a divisor of the uncompressed image size to the whished compressed image size. The absolute value ranges from 0 to 1000. If the value is 0 or 1000 then the loss-less variant of JPEG2000 compression is used (see also DynaPDF.SetJPEGQuality ). See also GetJPEGQuality function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.SetJPEGQuality This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 DynaPDF.GetIsTaggingEnabled - DynaPDF.GetJavaScript 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

functions.name: DynaPDF.GetJavaScript
functions.description:
Returns a global JavaScript as string. The parameter Index must be a valid handle of a global JavaScript or the name. To enumerate all JavaScripts of a document use the function DynaPDF.GetJavaScriptCount and then run a loop calling this function. See also GetJavaScript function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Query the JavaScript of the thing we added: Copy Text Copy XML Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.AddJavaScript "; $pdf ; "test"; "function test() { return 123; }") ] Set Variable [ $name ; Value: MBS ("DynaPDF.GetJavaScript"; $pdf ; "test") ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.AddJavaScript"; $pdf; "test"; "function test() { return 123; }") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.GetJavaScript"; $pdf; "test") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$name</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> See also DynaPDF.AddJavaScript DynaPDF.ChangeJavaScript DynaPDF.ChangeJavaScriptAction DynaPDF.ChangeJavaScriptName DynaPDF.CheckFieldNames DynaPDF.DeleteJavaScripts DynaPDF.GetJavaScriptAction DynaPDF.GetJavaScriptName DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.SetFieldName Release notes Version 13.2 Added DynaPDF.AddJavaScript , DynaPDF.ChangeJavaScript , DynaPDF.ChangeJavaScriptAction , DynaPDF.ChangeJavaScriptName , DynaPDF.GetActionCount , DynaPDF.GetJavaScript , DynaPDF.GetJavaScriptAction , DynaPDF.GetJavaScriptName . functions.name: DynaPDF.GetLastTextPosX
functions.description: Return the horizontal end position of the last drawn text.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

functions.name: DynaPDF.GetLastTextPosXAbs
functions.description: The function returns the x coordinate of the last printed text. This function returns the absolute end position of the last drawn text after applying coordinate transformations, if any. The relative end point before applying coordinate transformations is returned by DynaPDF.GetLastTextPosX / DynaPDF.GetLastTextPosY . The y-coordinate is the position of the text's baseline measured in bottom-up coordinates. Note that the reference point of DynaPDF.WriteFText is the baseline plus font size, independent of the current coordinate system. In contrast, the reference point of DynaPDF.WriteFText is the baseline if bottom up coordinates are active and the baseline plus font size if top down coordinates are active (see DynaPDF.SetPageCoords for further information). If the function succeeds the return value is the x- or y-coordinate. This value can be negative depending on the coordinate where the string was placed. If the function fails the return value is a negative error code. An error code is smaller than -33554531.0.

If the function succeeds the return value is the x- or y-coordinate. This value can be negative depending on the coordinate where the string was placed. functions.name: DynaPDF.GetLeading
functions.description: The function returns the current leading. The leading is the distance between two text lines. The default leading in PDF is the font size. The property is used by the function DynaPDF.AddContinueText and DynaPDF.WriteFText . A value of 0 determines that the font size should be used as leading, this is the default behavior. Default value = 0 See also GetLeading function in DynaPDF manual. functions.name: DynaPDF.GetLogMetafileSize
functions.description: Queries bounding rectangle from EMF file. Retrieves the logical bounding box of an enhanced or Windows metafile. The parameter FileName must be the file path to the EMF or WMF file. Returns the unscaled logical bounding box of the metafile. This bounding box is required to calculate a user defined cutting area or viewport (see the example below the description of the function). Two WMF formats are available, the old non-portable WMF format and the newer portable WMF format. Both formats must be converted to EMF with the GDI function SetWinMetaFileBits() before the logical bounding box can be calculated (the conversion is done automatically). However, non- portable WMF files are device-dependent, they contain no size information. To get correct results, the default size must be set with the function DynaPDF.SetWMFDefExtent beforehand. The parameters Width and Height are passed to the member xExt, yExt of the structure METAFILEPICT which is required to convert WMF files to EMF. The default size is 0, 0, that means the GDI calculates the size automatically but mostly incorrect. Because the GDI function SetWinMeatFileBits() is used to convert WMF files to EMF, WMF files are not supported under Linux or UNIX. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer. See also GetLogMetafileSize function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.GetLogMetafileSizeEx DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.SetWMFDefExtent Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath This function checks for a license. Created 18th May 2017 , last changed 18th May 2017 DynaPDF.GetLinkHighlightMode - DynaPDF.GetLogMetafileSizeEx 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: DynaPDF.GetMetadata
functions.description: Queries metadata from PDF. Reads the optional metadata streams of pages, fonts, images, pages, templates, as well as the global metadata stream that is associated with the Catalog object. Metadata streams are in XMP format that is a superset of XML. The global metadata stream will be created when this function is called. The returned stream is a preview of the XMP stream that will be stored in the file when DynaPDF.CloseFile or DynaPDF.CloseFileEx is called. Note that the creation and modification date will be updated when the file is closed (except if the stream will be modified or replaced with DynaPDF.SetMetadata ). The original global XMP stream of external PDF files can be accessed with DynaPDF.GetInMetadata . Metadata streams are optional and maybe not present. The function returns true if no error occurs, also if no metadata stream is present. The caller must also check whether the text returned has a length greater than zero to determine whether stream data was returned. See also GetMetadata function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.GetInMetadata DynaPDF.GetLeading DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.SetMetadata Blog Entries MBS FileMaker Plugin, version 5.2pr9 This function checks for a license. Created 29th July 2015 , last changed 29th July 2015 DynaPDF.GetMetaConvFlags - DynaPDF.GetNeedAppearance 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: DynaPDF.GetOCG
functions.description: Returns properties for layer. returns the properties of the Content Usage dictionary that is associated with an OCG or layer. If the OCG is not included in an application event, then the contents in this dictionary serves as pure information. If the OCG is included in one or more application events, then these settings control also the visibility state of the OCG. The function DynaPDF.GetOCG returns the events and categories which control the layer visibility. If UserNamesCount is greater zero, the dictionary contains also user names. The user names can be accessed with DynaPDF.GetOCGUsageUserName . Properties:

parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

examples.label: Find layers in PDF:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$pdf
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.New
") ]
#
# import file
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer
";
$pdf
;
Layers::Input
PDF) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage
";
$pdf
; 1) ]
#
# look for layers
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:
MBS
( "
DynaPDF.GetOCGCount
" ;
$pdf
) ]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value: 0 ]
Loop
Exit Loop If [
$index
≥
$count
]
#
Set Variable [
$Name
; Value:
MBS
( "DynaPDF.GetOCG" ;
$pdf
;
$index
; "Name" ) ]
Set Variable [
$Intent
; Value:
MBS
( "DynaPDF.GetOCG" ;
$pdf
;
$index
; "Intent" ) ]
Set Variable [
$Visible
; Value:
MBS
( "
Math.BitwiseAND
";
$intent
; 32 ) = 32 ]
#
New Record/Request
Set Field [
Layers::Name
;
$name
]
Set Field [
Layers::Visible
;
$visible
]
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: Off ]
#
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+ 1 ]
End Loop
#
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
DynaPDF.Release
";
$pdf
) ]

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.New") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pdf</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> import file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; $pdf; Layers::Input PDF) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage"; $pdf; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> look for layers</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS ( "DynaPDF.GetOCGCount" ; $pdf ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $index ≥ $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS ( "DynaPDF.GetOCG" ; $pdf ; $index; "Name" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Name</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS ( "DynaPDF.GetOCG" ; $pdf ; $index; "Intent" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Intent</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Math.BitwiseAND"; $intent; 32 ) = 32 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Visible</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$name </Calculation>
    <Field table="Layers" id="99999" name="Name "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$visible </Calculation>
    <Field table="Layers" id="99999" name="Visible "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.Release"; $pdf ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DynaPDF.GetOpacity
functions.description: Returns the opacity value used to draw the visible appearance of an annotation (requires Acrobat 5 or higher). A value of 1 determines that the annotation is completely opaque. If the value is zero, newly created annotations will be invisible. The opacity flag is not supported by all annotation types. At time of publication the opacity property is used for annotations only. Later versions of DynaPDF use this property may be for other PDF objects too. See also GetOpacity function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.GetLeading DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.SetOpacity This function checks for a license. functions.name: DynaPDF.GetOrientation
functions.description:
Queries orientation of current page. The function returns the orientation of the current open page if an open page was detected or the default orientation for newly created pages if no open page was detected. The orientation is measured in degrees; it is also always a multiple of 90 or 0. See also GetOrientation function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Rotate coordinate system as needed: Copy Text Copy XML Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetOrientationEx "; $pdf ; MBS ("DynaPDF.GetOrientation"; $pdf )) ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SetOrientationEx"; $pdf; MBS("DynaPDF.GetOrientation"; $pdf)) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> See also DynaPDF.GetAnnotation DynaPDF.GetImportOrientation DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.Parser.DeleteText DynaPDF.Parser.FindText DynaPDF.RotateCoords DynaPDF.RotateTemplate DynaPDF.SetFieldOrientation DynaPDF.SetOrientation DynaPDF.SetOrientationEx Example Databases DynaPDF/Find and Replace Text Blog Entries Retract text from a PDF with DynaPDF This function checks for a license. functions.name: DynaPDF.GetOutputIntent
functions.description: Returns an output intent as well as the properties of it. An output intent is an ICC profile that describes the characteristics of the output device. The profile is mostly embedded but certain standards like PDF/X 4p, for example, allow also the usage of non-embedded profiles. The parameter Index is the array index of the output intent. To determine the number of available intents call DynaPDF.GetOutputIntentCount . See also GetOutputIntent function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.AddOutputIntent DynaPDF.AddOutputIntentEx DynaPDF.DeleteOutputIntent DynaPDF.GetOutputIntentCount DynaPDF.New Blog Entries MBS FileMaker Plugin, version 5.2pr9 This function checks for a license. functions.name: DynaPDF.GetPageCount
functions.description:
Queries number of pages in the current PDF. Please note that we have DynaPDF.GetPageCount for the current working PDF and the DynaPDF.GetImportPageCount for the current open import PDF. See also GetPageCount function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Queries number of pages in current working PDF: Copy Text Copy XML $PageCount = MBS ( "DynaPDF.GetPageCount"; $PDF ) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.GetPageCount"; $PDF )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$PageCount</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Import PDF pages from 2 documents: Copy Text Copy XML # load PDF from first container Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer "; $pdf ; Test::pdf1 ) ] # import all pages from first container Set Variable [ $destPage ; Value: MBS ( "DynaPDF.GetPageCount"; PDF ) + 1 ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile "; $pdf ; $destPage ) ] # load PDF from second container Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer "; $pdf ; Test::pdf2 ) ] # import all pages from second container Set Variable [ $destPage ; Value: MBS ( "DynaPDF.GetPageCount"; PDF ) + 1 ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile "; $pdf ; $destPage ) ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> load PDF from first container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; $pdf; Test::pdf1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> import all pages from first container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.GetPageCount"; PDF ) + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$destPage</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile"; $pdf; $destPage) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> load PDF from second container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; $pdf; Test::pdf2) </Calculation>

    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

    </Repetition>
