functions.name: DynaPDF.InsertBookmark
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.InsertBookmark"; PDF; Title; Parent; DestPage { ; Open; AddChildren } )
functions.description: Inserts a bookmark in an existing outline tree. It sets also the page mode to UseOutlines (see DynaPDF.SetPageMode ). If the outline tree should not be shown when opening the document then set the page mode back to UseNone or another value before closing the document. Parent can be the handle of the parent bookmark after the new bookmark should be inserted or -1. If Parent is set to -1 the bookmark is inserted as the first root node in the outline tree. If the parameter AddChildren is set to true, the bookmarks below the new one are added as children to the new bookmark. This can be useful when merging PDF files and if the bookmarks from each PDF file should be separated. Notice: When inserting a bookmark the handles of all bookmarks below the new one are incremented by one. See also InsertBookmark function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.AddBookmark DynaPDF.ChangeBookmark DynaPDF.DeleteBookmark DynaPDF.FindNextBookmark DynaPDF.GetBookmark DynaPDF.InsertBookmarkEx DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.SetPageMode This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 DynaPDF.InsertBarcode - DynaPDF.InsertBookmarkEx 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: Bookmark title

parameters.name: Parent
parameters.description: Immediate parent bookmark

parameters.name: DestPage
parameters.description: Destination page that should be opened.

parameters.name: Open
parameters.description: Optionally, open or close the node when it contains children. Default 0.

parameters.name: AddChildren
parameters.description: Optionally, add all bookmarks below the new one as children. Default 0.

functions.result: Returns new bookmark handle or error.

functions.name: DynaPDF.InsertBookmarkEx
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.InsertBookmarkEx"; PDF; Title; Parent; NamedDest { ; Open; AddChildren } )
functions.description: Inserts a bookmark in an existing outline tree. It sets also the page mode to UseOutlines (see DynaPDF.SetPageMode ). If the outline tree should not be shown when opening the document then set the page mode back to UseNone or another value before closing the document. Parent can be the handle of the parent bookmark after the new bookmark should be inserted or -1. If Parent is set to -1 the bookmark is inserted as the first root node in the outline tree. If the parameter AddChildren is set to 1, the bookmarks below the new one are added as children to the new bookmark. This can be useful when merging PDF files and if the bookmarks from each PDF file should be separated. NamedDest must be a valid handle from a named destination, see DynaPDF.CreateNamedDest for further information. If the destination lies in another document then insert the bookmark with DynaPDF.InsertBookmark , create an extended Go To Remote Action with DynaPDF.CreateGoToRActionEx , and add the action finally to the bookmark with DynaPDF.AddActionToObj . Notice: When inserting a bookmark the handles of all bookmarks below the new one are incremented by one. See also InsertBookmarkEx function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.AddActionToObj DynaPDF.AddBookmark DynaPDF.AddBookmarkEx DynaPDF.CreateGoToRAction DynaPDF.CreateGoToRActionEx DynaPDF.CreateNamedDest DynaPDF.FindNextBookmark DynaPDF.InsertBookmark DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.SetPageMode This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 DynaPDF.InsertBookmark - DynaPDF.InsertImage 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: The Bookmark title.

parameters.name: Parent
parameters.description: Immediate parent bookmark. Must be a valid bookmark handle or -1.

parameters.name: NamedDest
parameters.description: Named Destination handle

parameters.name: Open
parameters.description: Optional, whether to open or close the node when it contains children. Default is 0 to close.

parameters.name: AddChildren
parameters.description: Optionally, add all bookmarks below the new one as children. Default 0.

functions.result: Returns new bookmark handle or error.

functions.name: DynaPDF.InsertImage
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.InsertImage"; PDF; Container; PosX; PosY; ScaleWidth; ScaleHeight { ; Index } )
functions.description:
The function inserts an image from a container. Same as DynaPDF.InsertImageFile , but reading from container. A specific image of a multi-page image can be selected with the parameter Index; the first image is denoted by the index 1. If the image file is not a multi-page image, the parameter Index is ignored. To determine the number of images stored in an image file use the function DynaPDF.GetImageCount . The usage of the function is described in detail at DynaPDF.InsertImageFile . Please use DynaPDF.SetCompressionFilter to define the compression of your images. Use "flate" there for no jpeg compression. Use DynaPDF.SetJPEGQuality to define JPEG compression quality. also use DynaPDF.SetSaveNewImageFormat with parameter 0 to embed JPEGs to PDF without recompression. Also you should turn off transparency by key color using DynaPDF.SetUseTransparency . You can use DynaPDF.SetAlpha to set fill transparency to affect how images gain extra transparency. Coordinates in PDF are by default in a 72dpi raster with lower left being 0/0 point. You can query page size by DynaPDF.GetPageWidth and DynaPDF.GetPageHeight functions. For all image operation, it is recommended to initialize color management with DynaPDF.InitColorManagement function. See also InsertImage function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Set a few options and insert image from file: Copy Text Copy XML MBS ( " DynaPDF.SetCompressionFilter "; $PDF ; "flate" ) MBS ( " DynaPDF.SetSaveNewImageFormat "; $PDF ; 0 ) MBS ( " DynaPDF.SetResolution "; $PDF ; 300 ) MBS ( " DynaPDF.SetUseTransparency "; $PDF ; 0 ) MBS ( "DynaPDF.InsertImage"; $PDF ; MyDatabase::ImageContainer ; 72; 72; 300; 200; 1 ) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.SetCompressionFilter"; $PDF; "flate" )
MBS( "DynaPDF.SetSaveNewImageFormat"; $PDF; 0 )
MBS( "DynaPDF.SetResolution"; $PDF; 300 )
MBS( "DynaPDF.SetUseTransparency"; $PDF; 0 )
MBS( "DynaPDF.InsertImage"; $PDF; MyDatabase::ImageContainer; 72; 72; 300; 200; 1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Insert and reduce qualty to save space: Copy Text Copy XML MBS ( " DynaPDF.SetJPEGQuality "; $PDF ; 75 ) MBS ( " DynaPDF.SetCompressionFilter "; $PDF ; "jpeg" ) MBS ( " DynaPDF.SetSaveNewImageFormat "; $PDF ; 1 ) MBS ( " DynaPDF.SetResolution "; $PDF ; 100 ) MBS ( " DynaPDF.SetUseTransparency "; $PDF ; 0 ) MBS ( "DynaPDF.InsertImage"; $PDF ; MyDatabase::ImageContainer ; 72; 72; 300; 200; 1 ) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.SetJPEGQuality"; $PDF; 75 )
MBS( "DynaPDF.SetCompressionFilter"; $PDF; "jpeg" )
MBS( "DynaPDF.SetSaveNewImageFormat"; $PDF; 1 )
MBS( "DynaPDF.SetResolution"; $PDF; 100 )
MBS( "DynaPDF.SetUseTransparency"; $PDF; 0 )
MBS( "DynaPDF.InsertImage"; $PDF; MyDatabase::ImageContainer; 72; 72; 300; 200; 1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Loop over all pages and insert an image on each page: Copy Text Copy XML # Initialize DynaPDF if needed If [ MBS (" DynaPDF.IsInitialized ")  ≠  1 ] Perform Script [ Specified: From list ; “InitDynaPDF” ; Parameter:    ] End If # Start new PDF environment Set Variable [ $pdf ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.New ") ] # Load PDF from container Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer "; $pdf ; Watermark pages::InputPDF ) ] # import a page Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile "; $pdf ) ] # now edit all pages Set Variable [ $count ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.GetPageCount "; $pdf ) ] Set Variable [ $index ; Value: 1 ] Loop Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.EditPage "; $pdf ; $index ) ] Set Variable [ $index ; Value: $index + 1 ] # Options for saving image Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetSaveNewImageFormat "; $pdf ; 1) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetCompressionFilter "; $pdf ; "jpeg") ] # Insert image Set Variable [ $w ; Value: 200 ] Set Variable [ $h ; Value: 200 ] Set Variable [ $x ; Value: ( MBS (" DynaPDF.GetPageWidth "; $pdf ) - $w )/2 ] Set Variable [ $y ; Value: ( MBS (" DynaPDF.GetPageHeight "; $pdf ) - $h )/2 ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ("DynaPDF.InsertImage"; $pdf ; Watermark pages::WatermarkPicture ; $x ; $y ; $w ; $h ) ] # End page Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.EndPage "; $pdf ) ] Exit Loop If [ $count < $index ] End Loop # Set title Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetDocInfo "; $pdf ; "Title"; "Test PDF") ] # Save Set Variable [ $PDFData ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Save "; $pdf ; "hello.pdf") ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Release "; $pdf ) ] # Put in Container Set Field [ Watermark pages::OutputPDF ; $PDFData ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Initialize DynaPDF if needed</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("DynaPDF.IsInitialized")  ≠  1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="1" name="Perform Script">
    <Calculation>  </Calculation>
    <Script id="99999" name="InitDynaPDF"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Start new PDF environment</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.New") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pdf</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Load PDF from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; $pdf; Watermark pages::InputPDF) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> import a page</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now edit all pages</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.GetPageCount"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.EditPage"; $pdf; $index) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Options for saving image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SetSaveNewImageFormat"; $pdf; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SetCompressionFilter"; $pdf; "jpeg") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Insert image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>200 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$w</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>200 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$h</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>(MBS("DynaPDF.GetPageWidth"; $pdf) - $w)/2 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$x</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>(MBS("DynaPDF.GetPageHeight"; $pdf) - $h)/2 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$y</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.InsertImage"; $pdf; Watermark pages::WatermarkPicture; $x; $y; $w; $h) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> End page</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.EndPage"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $count < $index </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Set title</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SetDocInfo"; $pdf; "Title"; "Test PDF") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Save</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.Save"; $pdf; "hello.pdf") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$PDFData</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.Release"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Put in Container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$PDFData </Calculation>
    <Field table="Watermark pages" id="99999" name="OutputPDF "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Place an image centered on the page with border: Copy Text Copy XML Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.AppendPage "; $pdf ) ] # put a background in Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetFillColor "; $pdf ; ,8; ,8; ,8) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Rectangle "; $pdf ; 0; 0; MBS ( " DynaPDF.GetPageWidth "; $PDF ); MBS ( " DynaPDF.GetPageHeight "; $PDF ); "fill") ] # options for saving image Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetSaveNewImageFormat "; $pdf ; 0) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetResolution "; $pdf ; 300) ] # read size of image Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ReadImageFormat "; $pdf ; Merge PDFs::InputImage ) ] Set Variable [ $ImageWidth ; Value: GetAsNumber (LeftValues ( $r ; 1 )) ] Set Variable [ $imageHeight ; Value: GetAsNumber (MiddleValues ( $r ; 2; 1 )) ] # The destination area where to put PDF Set Variable [ $TargetX ; Value: 50 ] Set Variable [ $TargetY ; Value: 50 ] Set Variable [ $TargetWidth ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.GetPageWidth "; $PDF ) - 100 ] Set Variable [ $TargetHeight ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.GetPageHeight "; $PDF ) - 100 ] # calculate scale factor Set Variable [ $factor ; Value: Min( $TargetHeight / $ImageHeight ; $TargetWidth / $ImageWidth ) ] # calculate output size Set Variable [ $DestWidth ; Value: $ImageWidth * $factor ] Set Variable [ $DestHeight ; Value: $ImageHeight * $factor ] # Center in target area Set Variable [ $DestX ; Value: $TargetX + ( $TargetWidth - $DestWidth ) / 2 ] Set Variable [ $DestY ; Value: $TargetY + ( $TargetHeight - $DestHeight ) / 2 ] # insert the image Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ("DynaPDF.InsertImage"; $pdf ; Merge PDFs::InputImage ; $DestX ; $DestY ; $DestWidth ; $DestHeight ) ] # close page and move to next record Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.EndPage "; $pdf ) ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.AppendPage"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> put a background in</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SetFillColor"; $pdf; ,8; ,8; ,8) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.Rectangle"; $pdf; 0; 0; MBS( "DynaPDF.GetPageWidth"; $PDF ); MBS( "DynaPDF.GetPageHeight"; $PDF ); "fill") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> options for saving image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SetSaveNewImageFormat"; $pdf; 0) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SetResolution"; $pdf; 300) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> read size of image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ReadImageFormat"; $pdf; Merge PDFs::InputImage) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetAsNumber (LeftValues ( $r ; 1 )) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ImageWidth</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetAsNumber (MiddleValues ( $r ; 2; 1 )) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$imageHeight</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> The destination area where to put PDF</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>50 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$TargetX</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>50 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$TargetY</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.GetPageWidth"; $PDF ) - 100 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$TargetWidth</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.GetPageHeight"; $PDF ) - 100 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$TargetHeight</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> calculate scale factor</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Min( $TargetHeight / $ImageHeight; $TargetWidth / $ImageWidth) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$factor</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> calculate output size</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$ImageWidth * $factor </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$DestWidth</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$ImageHeight * $factor </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$DestHeight</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Center in target area</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$TargetX + ($TargetWidth-$DestWidth) / 2 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$DestX</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$TargetY + ($TargetHeight-$DestHeight) / 2 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$DestY</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> insert the image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.InsertImage"; $pdf; Merge PDFs::InputImage; $DestX; $DestY; $DestWidth; $DestHeight) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> close page and move to next record</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.EndPage"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> See also DynaPDF.InsertImageFile DynaPDF.ListImages DynaPDF.SetAlpha DynaPDF.SetCompressionLevel DynaPDF.SetFillColor DynaPDF.SetJPEGQuality DynaPDF.SetRenderingIntent DynaPDF.SetResolution DynaPDF.SetUseTransparency OCR.WriteToPDF Release notes Version 14.5 Improved DynaPDF.GeneratePreview , DynaPDF.ReadImageResolution , DynaPDF.ReadImageFormat , DynaPDF.InsertImage , DynaPDF.AttachFileContainer , DynaPDF.AddOutputIntentEx , DynaPDF.CreateICCBasedColorSpaceContainer and DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer to return proper error if container is empty. parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The image container value. Pass the container field or a variable with the container content.

parameters.name: PosX
parameters.description: The X position (from left of page)

parameters.name: PosY
parameters.description: The Y position (from bottom of page)

parameters.name: ScaleWidth
parameters.description: The scaled width of the image.

parameters.name: ScaleHeight
parameters.description: The scaled height of the image.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the image for multi image files.

functions.result: Returns image handle number or error message.

examples.label: Loop over all pages and insert an image on each page:
examples.text_code:
# Initialize DynaPDF if needed
If [
MBS
("
DynaPDF.IsInitialized
")  ≠  1 ]
Perform Script [ Specified: From list ; “InitDynaPDF” ; Parameter:    ]
End If
# Start new PDF environment
Set Variable [
$pdf
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.New
") ]
# Load PDF from container
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer
";
$pdf
;
Watermark pages::InputPDF
) ]
# import a page
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile
";
$pdf
) ]
# now edit all pages
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.GetPageCount
";
$pdf
) ]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value: 1 ]
Loop
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.EditPage
";
$pdf
;
$index
) ]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+ 1 ]
# Options for saving image
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.SetSaveNewImageFormat
";
$pdf
; 1) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.SetCompressionFilter
";
$pdf
; "jpeg") ]
# Insert image
Set Variable [
$w
; Value: 200 ]
Set Variable [
$h
; Value: 200 ]
Set Variable [
$x
; Value: (
MBS
("
DynaPDF.GetPageWidth
";
$pdf
) -
$w
)/2 ]
Set Variable [
$y
; Value: (
MBS
("
DynaPDF.GetPageHeight
";
$pdf
) -
$h
)/2 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("DynaPDF.InsertImage";
$pdf
;
Watermark pages::WatermarkPicture
;
$x
;
$y
;
$w
;
$h
) ]
# End page
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.EndPage
";
$pdf
) ]
Exit Loop If [
$count
<
$index
]
End Loop
# Set title
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.SetDocInfo
";
$pdf
; "Title"; "Test PDF") ]
# Save
Set Variable [
$PDFData
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.Save
";
$pdf
; "hello.pdf") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.Release
";
$pdf
) ]
# Put in Container
Set Field [
Watermark pages::OutputPDF
;
$PDFData
]

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; $pdf; Watermark pages::InputPDF) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now edit all pages</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>1 </Calculation>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.EditPage"; $pdf; $index) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Options for saving image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SetSaveNewImageFormat"; $pdf; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SetCompressionFilter"; $pdf; "jpeg") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Insert image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>200 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$w</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>200 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$h</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>(MBS("DynaPDF.GetPageWidth"; $pdf) - $w)/2 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$x</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>(MBS("DynaPDF.GetPageHeight"; $pdf) - $h)/2 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$y</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.InsertImage"; $pdf; Watermark pages::WatermarkPicture; $x; $y; $w; $h) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> End page</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.EndPage"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $count < $index </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Set title</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SetDocInfo"; $pdf; "Title"; "Test PDF") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Save</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.Save"; $pdf; "hello.pdf") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$PDFData</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.Release"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Put in Container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$PDFData </Calculation>
    <Field table="Watermark pages" id="99999" name="OutputPDF "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

; ,8; ,8; ,8) ]

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SetResolution"; $pdf; 300) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> read size of image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ReadImageFormat"; $pdf; Merge PDFs::InputImage) </Calculation>

    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetAsNumber (LeftValues ( $r ; 1 )) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ImageWidth</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetAsNumber (MiddleValues ( $r ; 2; 1 )) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$imageHeight</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> The destination area where to put PDF</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>50 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$TargetX</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>50 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$TargetY</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.GetPageWidth"; $PDF ) - 100 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$TargetWidth</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.GetPageHeight"; $PDF ) - 100 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$TargetHeight</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> calculate scale factor</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

    </Repetition>

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$TargetY + ($TargetHeight-$DestHeight) / 2 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$DestY</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> insert the image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.InsertImage"; $pdf; Merge PDFs::InputImage; $DestX; $DestY; $DestWidth; $DestHeight) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> close page and move to next record</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.EndPage"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DynaPDF.InsertImageFile
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.InsertImageFile"; PDF; FilePath; PosX; PosY; ScaleWidth; ScaleHeight { ; Index } )
functions.description:
The function inserts an image from a file. The parameter Index specifies the image index of a multi- page image. The first image index is denoted by 1. If the image file is not a multi-page image, the parameter Index will be ignored. To determine the number of images stored in an image file call the function DynaPDF.GetImageCount . Positions and sizes are in PDF coordinates which normally is 72 dpi. Please use DynaPDF.SetCompressionFilter to define the compression of your images. Use "flate" there for no jpeg compression. Use DynaPDF.SetJPEGQuality to define JPEG compression quality. also use DynaPDF.SetSaveNewImageFormat with parameter 0 to embed JPEGs to PDF without recompression. Also you should turn off transparency by key color using DynaPDF.SetUseTransparency . You can use DynaPDF.SetAlpha to set fill transparency to affect how images gain extra transparency. Coordinates in PDF are by default in a 72dpi raster with lower left being 0/0 point. You can query page size by DynaPDF.GetPageWidth and DynaPDF.GetPageHeight functions. See also InsertImageFile function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Set a few options and insert image from file: Copy Text Copy XML MBS ( " DynaPDF.SetCompressionFilter "; $PDF ; "flate" ) MBS ( " DynaPDF.SetSaveNewImageFormat "; $PDF ; 0 ) MBS ( " DynaPDF.SetResolution "; $PDF ; 300 ) MBS ( " DynaPDF.SetUseTransparency "; $PDF ; 0 ) MBS ( "DynaPDF.InsertImageFile"; $PDF ; "C:\test.jpg"; 72; 72; 300; 200; 1 ) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.SetCompressionFilter"; $PDF; "flate" )
MBS( "DynaPDF.SetSaveNewImageFormat"; $PDF; 0 )
MBS( "DynaPDF.SetResolution"; $PDF; 300 )
MBS( "DynaPDF.SetUseTransparency"; $PDF; 0 )
MBS( "DynaPDF.InsertImageFile"; $PDF; "C:\test.jpg"; 72; 72; 300; 200; 1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Insert and reduce qualty to save space: Copy Text Copy XML MBS ( " DynaPDF.SetJPEGQuality "; $PDF ; 75 ) MBS ( " DynaPDF.SetCompressionFilter "; $PDF ; "jpeg" ) MBS ( " DynaPDF.SetSaveNewImageFormat "; $PDF ; 1 ) MBS ( " DynaPDF.SetResolution "; $PDF ; 100 ) MBS ( " DynaPDF.SetUseTransparency "; $PDF ; 0 ) MBS ( "DynaPDF.InsertImageFile"; $PDF ; "C:\test.jpg"; 72; 72; 300; 200; 1 ) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.SetJPEGQuality"; $PDF; 75 )
MBS( "DynaPDF.SetCompressionFilter"; $PDF; "jpeg" )
MBS( "DynaPDF.SetSaveNewImageFormat"; $PDF; 1 )
MBS( "DynaPDF.SetResolution"; $PDF; 100 )
MBS( "DynaPDF.SetUseTransparency"; $PDF; 0 )
MBS( "DynaPDF.InsertImageFile"; $PDF; "C:\test.jpg"; 72; 72; 300; 200; 1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> See also DynaPDF.AppendImagePages DynaPDF.GetPageHeight DynaPDF.GetPageWidth DynaPDF.InsertImage DynaPDF.InsertMetafile DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.SetAlpha DynaPDF.SetJPEGQuality DynaPDF.SetResolution DynaPDF.SetUseTransparency Release notes Version 7.2 Fixed a bug with DynaPDF.InsertImageFile . Example Databases Mac and iOS/PDFKit/Pictures to PDF Blog Entries DynaPDF graphics flags Things you can do with DynaPDF MBS FileMaker Plugin, version 7.2pr1 MBS FileMaker Plugin, version 5.0pr10 FileMaker Magazin Ausgabe 5/2021, Seite 26 This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 2nd September 2022 DynaPDF.InsertImage - DynaPDF.InsertMetafile 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: DynaPDF.InsertMetafile
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.InsertMetafile"; PDF; FileName; PosX; PosY; Width; Height { ; ViewLeft; ViewTop; ViewRight; ViewBottom } )
functions.description: Inserts metafile as vector graphics. The function converts an Enhanced Meta File (EMF) or Windows Meta File (WMF) to native PDF vector graphics. EMF+ files do not work. Requires DynaPDF Lite license. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer. See also InsertMetafile function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.GetWMFDefExtent DynaPDF.GetWMFPixelPerInch DynaPDF.Initialize DynaPDF.InsertImageFile DynaPDF.InsertMetafileEx DynaPDF.New Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath This function checks for a license. Created 18th May 2017 , last changed 27th July 2018 DynaPDF.InsertImageFile - DynaPDF.InsertMetafileEx 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: DynaPDF.IsColorPage
functions.description:
Checks whether a page is a color page or if all graphic elements of the page use black & white only. If the parameter GrayIsColor is true, gray shades are treated as color. The page which should be checked must be opened with the function DynaPDF.EditPage beforehand. Complex color spaces such as Lab, Separation, DeviceN, Colored Tiling Patterns, and Shadings are always treated as color. The function does not return when a color is set, the color must be used by an object. If an object of the page uses a color, the return value is 1. If no object uses a color the return value is 0. See also DynaPDF.IsEmptyPage . See also IsColorPage function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Check if first page has color: Copy Text Copy XML Set Variable [ $pdf ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.New ") ] # Load PDF from container Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer "; $pdf ; Start::SourcePDF ) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile "; $pdf ) ] # Query image list Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.EditPage "; $pdf ; 1) ] Show Custom Dialog [ MBS ( "DynaPDF.IsColorPage"; $PDF ) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.EndPage "; $pdf ) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Release "; $pdf ) ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

    </Value>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">

functions.name: DynaPDF.IsEmptyPage
functions.description: Checks whether a page is empty. The page which should be checked must be opened with the function DynaPDF.EditPage beforehand. The function does not check whether the buffer size of a page is zero to determine whether a page is empty. Instead, the content stream will be parsed until a visible object can be found. If no visible object can be found the return value is 1. If a visible object can be found the return value is 0. See also IsEmptyPage function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.EditPage DynaPDF.IsColorPage DynaPDF.New Blog Entries MBS FileMaker Plugin, version 6.5pr2 This function checks for a license. Created 6th February 2015 , last changed 5th October 2016 DynaPDF.IsColorPage - DynaPDF.IsInitialized 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: DynaPDF.IsInitialized
functions.description: Checks if DynaPDF library has been initialized. If value is 1, everything is okay. If value is zero, the library is not initialized and you need to call DynaPDF.Initialize function. On a hosted database this function is better than using a global variable, as the variable can't see if initialization was made on server or client side.

examples.label: Initialize when needed:
examples.text_code:
#Initialize DynaPDF
If [
MBS
( "DynaPDF.IsInitialized" )  ≠ 1]
If [Get ( SystemPlatform ) = -3]
# iOS
Exit Script []
Else If [Get ( SystemPlatform ) = -2]
# Windows
Set Variable [
$path
; Value: "dynapdf.dll"]
Else
# Mac
Set Variable [
$path
; Value: "dynapdf.dylib"]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
DynaPDF.Initialize
";
$path
; "12345..." )]
If [
$r
≠ "OK"]
Show Custom Dialog [ "DynaPDF failed to initialize";
$r
]
End If
End If

functions.name: DynaPDF.Lab
functions.description: Calculates color value for LAB. For use with functions which take a color as a big number. Calculation is b * 65536 + a * 256 + L. Use it only if the color space is LAB. See also DynaPDF.RGB for RGB colors and DynaPDF.CMYK for CMYK colors.

parameters.name: L
parameters.description: The l color component from 0 to 100.

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: The a color component from -128 to 127.

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: The a color component from -128 to 127.

examples.label: Get Lab color value:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "DynaPDF.Lab"; 50; -34; 77 )
Example result:
5103154

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DynaPDF.LineAnnot
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.LineAnnot"; PDF; x1; y1; x2; y2; LineWidth; LineEndStyleStart; LineEndStyleEnd; FillColor; StrokeColor; ColorSpace { ; Author; Subject; Comment } )
functions.description: Creates a line annotation. The simplest form of a line annotation represents a simple straight line that has an associated PopUp annotation to display the string Content in a floating window. The initial window state of the associated PopUp annotation is closed by default but the state can be changed with DynaPDF.SetAnnotOpenState if necessary. The coordinates are interpreted in current user space. Any transformation that was applied on the coordinate system will be taken into account. The parameter FillColor is only used if the line end style of the start or end point has an interior that can be filled. The special constant NO_COLOR (4294967281) represents a transparent interior. The stroke color is required and must not be set to NO_COLOR (4294967281). Measure lines A measure line is an extended line annotation that has additional properties. To create a measure line create first the line annotation and set then the measure line specific properties with DynaPDF.SetLineAnnotParms. The parameter LineWidth must be in the range 0 through 12 units. Values outside the valid range will be adjusted to the nearest allowed value. A zero line width produces a 1 pixel wide line. The line end styles can be changed if necessary with DynaPDF.SetAnnotLineEndStyle . See also LineAnnot function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.CircleAnnot DynaPDF.FindLinkAnnot DynaPDF.LineTo DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.RGB DynaPDF.SetAnnotLineEndStyle DynaPDF.SetAnnotOpenState DynaPDF.SquareAnnot DynaPDF.StampAnnot DynaPDF.TextAnnot Blog Entries Unlocking the Power of PDFs in FileMaker with DynaPDF and the MBS FileMaker Plugin This function checks for a license. Created 28th November 2014 , last changed 17th July 2022 DynaPDF.Lab - DynaPDF.LineTo 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: DynaPDF.LineTo
functions.description:
The function draws a path from the current position up to the specified point. The start point must be set with another vector graphic function beforehand, such as DynaPDF.MoveTo or other functions which draw an open path segment. In PDF all vector graphics are defined as paths, a path is invisible as long it was not stroked, filled or both. See also Path construction and Painting. See also LineTo function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Draw two lines in black: Copy Text Copy XML Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.SetStrokeColor " ; $pdf ; 0; 0; 0 )] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.SetLineWidth " ; $pdf ; 1 )] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.MoveTo " ; $pdf ; 20; 20)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( "DynaPDF.LineTo" ; $pdf ; 20; 40)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.MoveTo " ; $pdf ; 30; 30)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( "DynaPDF.LineTo" ; $pdf ; 30; 50)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.StrokePath " ; $pdf )] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

functions.name: DynaPDF.LockLayer
functions.description: Adds a layer to the list of locked layers. The state of a locked layer cannot be changed through the user interface of a PDF viewer. However, the state of locked layers can still be changed via Javascript or DynaPDF.SetOCGState actions. See also LockLayer function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.BeginLayer DynaPDF.EndLayer DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.SetOCGState DynaPDF.UnLockLayer This function checks for a license. Created 6th February 2015 , last changed 6th February 2015 DynaPDF.ListImages - DynaPDF.MarkTemplateAsWatermark 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: DynaPDF.MarkTemplateAsWatermark
functions.description: Marks template as watermark. Adds additional metadata to a template (XObject type Form in PDF terms, see DynaPDF.BeginTemplate or DynaPDF.BeginTransparencyGroup for further information) so that PDF editors like Adobe Acrobat are able to identify the template as watermark. Watermarks can be deleted with Acrobat and many other PDF editors. DynaPDF can delete such watermarks too with DynaPDF.DeleteWatermark or DynaPDF.Optimize . See also MarkTemplateAsWatermark function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.BeginTemplate DynaPDF.DeleteWatermark DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.Optimize Release notes Version 13.3 Added DynaPDF.AddDPartNode , DynaPDF.CreateDPartRoot , DynaPDF.DeleteWatermark , DynaPDF.GetLastTextOffset , DynaPDF.HaveDPartRoot , DynaPDF.MarkTemplateAsWatermark and DynaPDF.OpenTagEx functions. Blog Entries New in MBS FileMaker Plugin 13.3 MBS FileMaker Plugin, version 13.3pr2 Created 13th June 2023 , last changed 13th June 2023 DynaPDF.LockLayer - DynaPDF.MovePage 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: DynaPDF.MovePage
functions.description: The function moves a page to another position in the document. Moving a page requires some processing time if the document contains many bookmarks or page links because the destinations of all links and bookmarks must be modified so that they refer still to the correct page. For example, if the first page of a document should be moved to the last page, all pages of the document must be reordered. If the document is large and if it contains a few thousands page links as well as bookmarks, this action can take a while. The parameter Source must be a valid page number in the document. Page numbering starts at one. If the destination page is larger than the source page, the page number can be greater than the number of pages currently available in the document. The document is then filled with empty pages until the destination page becomes valid. If the destination page is smaller than the source page number, both page numbers must already exist in the document. It is also possible to exchange two pages with DynaPDF.ExchangePage. You can combine DynaPDF.AppendPage and DynaPDF.MovePage to make an insert page function. See also MovePage function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.EditPage DynaPDF.EndPage DynaPDF.ExchangePages DynaPDF.MoveTo DynaPDF.New This function checks for a license. Created 2nd September 2014 , last changed 7th June 2024 DynaPDF.MarkTemplateAsWatermark - DynaPDF.MoveTo 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: DestPage

functions.name: DynaPDF.MoveTo

The function moves the current position to the point specified by PosX, PosY. This function must be called before a line or curved path segment can be drawn. See also DynaPDF.LineTo , DynaPDF.Bezier_1_2_3. See also MoveTo function in DynaPDF manual. functions.name: DynaPDF.New
functions.description:
Creates a new DynaPDF instance. You can have multiple instances per script and run multiple scripts parallel on server, but please only use one ID in one script at a time. Accessing the same DynaPDF object via ID in two parallel running scripts can lead to trouble. Must be paired with call to DynaPDF.Release to avoid running out of memory. If this function returns "OK", you have an older plugin version (before 3.5). For DynaPDF, you need an extra license as this is not included in the MBS FileMaker Plugin license. You can order DynaPDF add-on for our plugin from Monkeybread Software. Before you can create DynaPDF instances to work with the DynaPDF commands, you need to use DynaPDF.Initialize function to initialize the library. DynaPDF context reference numbers are starting at 20000 and counting up for each new context. Please use DynaPDF.AppendPage to create a new page. See also New function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Work with PDF: Copy Text Copy XML $pdf = MBS ("DynaPDF.New") # do your work $r = MBS ( " DynaPDF.Release "; $pdf ) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.New")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pdf</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> do your work</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.Release"; $pdf )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> See also DynaPDF.TranslateCoords DynaPDF.Triangle DynaPDF.UnLockLayer DynaPDF.WebLink DynaPDF.WriteDemoText DynaPDF.WriteFText DynaPDF.WriteFTextExRotated DynaPDF.WriteStyledText DynaPDF.WriteStyledTextEx DynaPDF.WriteStyledTextExRotated Release notes Version 11.3 Fixed a memory leak in DynaPDF.New function. Example Databases DynaPDF/Bookmarks DynaPDF/Change Separation Colorant DynaPDF/Convert to PDFA DynaPDF/Coordinates DynaPDF/DynaPDF Test DynaPDF/Form/Form DynaPDF/List Annotations DynaPDF/Page Layouting DynaPDF/Replace Image DynaPDF/Report Blog Entries Draw images with text using DynaPDF Retract text from a PDF with DynaPDF Use complex text rendering with DynaPDF in FileMaker How the Script Merges PDFs and Inserts an Image: A Detailed Breakdown MBS FileMaker Advent calendar - Door 10 - Reading The Wish List MBS FileMaker Advent calendar - Door 6 - Sending Out Wishlist Forms MBS FileMaker Advent calendar - Door 4 - Add GTC with DynaPDF Extract invoice for ZUGFeRD and Facture-X Unlocking the Power of PDFs in FileMaker with DynaPDF and the MBS FileMaker Plugin PDF Attachments in FileMaker with DynaPDF FileMaker Magazin Ausgabe 4/2022, Seite 33 Ausgabe 1/2022, Seite 22 Ausgabe 1/2022, Seite 19 bis 20 Ausgabe 5/2021, Seite 25 bis 26 Ausgabe 6/2020, Seite 34 Ausgabe 6/2020, Seite 32 Ausgabe 3/2019, Seite 36 Ausgabe 2/2019, Seite 25 bis 27 Ausgabe 5/2018, Seite 28 Ausgabe 1/2018, Seite 26 This function checks for a license. functions.name: DynaPDF.OpenOutputFile
functions.description:
The function opens the output file into which the PDF file should be written. The PDF file must be created in memory if this function should be used. This is the case if you called DynaPDF.New without a file path. It is strongly recommended to check with DynaPDF.HaveOpenDocument whether a PDF file is still in memory before calling this function. If you get back an error "Cannot open file!", please check if you didn't open the file before with DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile . In that case a call to DynaPDF.CloseImportFile may be needed. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer. See also OpenOutputFile function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Import selected pages from one PDF and write into a new one: Copy Text Copy XML # New PDF Set Variable [ $pdf ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.New ")] # Open existing PDF file (or use DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer for container) Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile "; $pdf ; $path )] # Query page count Set Variable [ $pageCount ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.GetImportPageCount "; $pdf )] # Import the pages you need Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage "; $pdf ; 1)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage "; $pdf ; 2)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage "; $pdf ; 3)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage "; $pdf ; $pagecount - 1)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage "; $pdf ; $pagecount - 2)] # Set output to this file path # MBS ( "DynaPDF.OpenOutputFile"; $pdf ; "/Users/cs/Desktop/output.pdf" ) # Write PDF Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Save "; $pdf )] # release memory Set Variable [ $r1 ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Release "; $pdf )] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

functions.name: DynaPDF.OpenOutputFileEncrypted
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.OpenOutputFileEncrypted"; PDF; Path; OpenPwd; OwnerPwd; Encryption { ; RestrictFlags } )
functions.description: The function opens the output file into which the PDF file should be written. The PDF file must be created in memory if this function should be used. This is the case if you called DynaPDF.New without a file path. It is strongly recommended to check with DynaPDF.HaveOpenDocument whether a PDF file is still in memory before calling this function. If input and output files should be the same, please make sure you close the import file first with DynaPDF.CloseImportFile function. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer. See also OpenOutputFileEncrypted function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.CloseImportFile DynaPDF.HaveOpenDocument DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.Save Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath Blog Entries MBS FileMaker Plugin, version 6.4pr1 This function checks for a license. Created 16th July 2016 , last changed 25th July 2023 DynaPDF.OpenOutputFile - DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The file path to create the PDF. The file name should include pdf file extension.

parameters.name: OpenPwd
parameters.description: Open password.

parameters.name: OwnerPwd
parameters.description: Owner password.

parameters.name: Encryption
parameters.description: Which encryption to use. Can be 40bit, 128bit, 128bitEx, AES128, AES256 or AESRev6.

parameters.name: RestrictFlags
parameters.description: What restrictions to apply. See above.

functions.name: DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer
functions.description:
Opens a PDF for importing from a container. Requires DynaPDF Lite license. On success returns a number ≥ 0. After opening the file, please use DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile or DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage to import one or more pages. Or just check metadata with e.g. DynaPDF.GetImportDocInfo and check page count and sizes with DynaPDF.GetImportPageBounds and DynaPDF.GetImportPageCount . If you use Container.ReadFile to read PDF and then pass it to DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer, please consider to use DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile instead to avoid extra memory usage and avoid FileMaker rendering a preview picture for the PDF. See also OpenPDFFromContainer function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Opens PDF: Copy Text Copy XML $r = MBS ( "DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; $PDF ; $PDFFile ) $error = MBS ( " DynaPDF.GetLastErrorMessage "; $PDF ) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; $PDF; $PDFFile )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.GetLastErrorMessage"; $PDF )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$error</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Load PDF and extract text of page 2: Copy Text Copy XML # initialize DynaPDF before running this: Set Variable [ $pdf ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.New ")] # load PDF from container Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; $pdf ; Test::data )] # import all pages Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile "; $pdf )] # extra text of page 2 Set Field [ Test::PageText ; MBS (" DynaPDF.ExtractPageText "; $pdf ; 2)] #Cleanup Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Release "; $pdf )] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> initialize DynaPDF before running this:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.New")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pdf</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> load PDF from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer";$pdf; Test::data)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> import all pages</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile";$pdf)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> extra text of page 2</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ExtractPageText"; $pdf; 2)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Test" id="99999" name="PageText"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.Release"; $pdf)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Import a single page: Copy Text Copy XML Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetImportFlags "; $pdf ; "ImportAll ImportAsPage")] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; $pdf ; Invoice::PDF Template)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage "; $pdf ; 1)] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SetImportFlags"; $pdf; "ImportAll ImportAsPage")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; $pdf; Invoice::PDF Template)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage"; $pdf; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Import selected pages from one PDF and write into a new one: Copy Text Copy XML # New PDF Set Variable [ $pdf ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.New ")] # Open existing PDF file (or use DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer for container) Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile "; $pdf ; $path )] # Query page count Set Variable [ $pageCount ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.GetImportPageCount "; $pdf )] # Import the pages you need Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage "; $pdf ; 1)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage "; $pdf ; 2)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage "; $pdf ; 3)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage "; $pdf ; $pagecount - 1)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage "; $pdf ; $pagecount - 2)] # Set output to this file path # MBS ( " DynaPDF.OpenOutputFile "; $pdf ; "/Users/cs/Desktop/output.pdf" ) # Write PDF Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Save "; $pdf )] # release memory Set Variable [ $r1 ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Release "; $pdf )] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> New PDF</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.New")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pdf</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Open existing PDF file (or use DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer for container)</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile"; $pdf; $path )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Query page count</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.GetImportPageCount"; $pdf )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pageCount</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Import the pages you need</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage"; $pdf; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage"; $pdf; 2)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage"; $pdf; 3)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage"; $pdf; $pagecount - 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage"; $pdf; $pagecount - 2)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Set output to this file path</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> MBS( "DynaPDF.OpenOutputFile"; $pdf; "/Users/cs/Desktop/output.pdf" )</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Write PDF</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.Save"; $pdf)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> release memory</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.Release"; $pdf)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r1</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Query width and height of first page in PDF: Copy Text Copy XML # Initialize DynaPDF if needed If [ MBS (" DynaPDF.IsInitialized ")  ≠  1 ] Perform Script [ Specified: From list ; “Initialize DynaPDF” ; Parameter:    ] End If # Clear current PDF document Set Variable [ $pdf ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.New ") ] # # Load PDF from container # Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; $pdf ; Test::PDF artwork) ] # # Query page size for the first page: # Set Variable [ $PageSize ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.GetImportPageBounds "; $pdf ; 1; "MediaBox") ] # # split numbers, may need special handling with Math.TextToNumber to make sure it works if you use comma as decimal separator: # Set Variable [ $left ; Value: MBS ( " Math.TextToNumber "; GetValue( $PageSize ; 1)) ] Set Variable [ $top ; Value: MBS ( " Math.TextToNumber "; GetValue( $PageSize ; 2)) ] Set Variable [ $right ; Value: MBS ( " Math.TextToNumber "; GetValue( $PageSize ; 3)) ] Set Variable [ $bottom ; Value: MBS ( " Math.TextToNumber "; GetValue( $PageSize ; 4)) ] # Set Variable [ $Width ; Value: Abs( $right - $left ) ] Set Variable [ $height ; Value: Abs( $top - $bottom ) ] # # Calculate in centimeters # Set Field [ Test::Job dimension horizontal ; Round( $Width * 2.54 / 72; 2) ] Set Field [ Test::Job dimension vertical ; Round( $Height * 2.54 / 72; 2) ] # # Cleanup Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Release "; $pdf ) ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Initialize DynaPDF if needed</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("DynaPDF.IsInitialized")  ≠  1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="1" name="Perform Script">
    <Calculation>  </Calculation>
    <Script id="99999" name="Initialize DynaPDF"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Clear current PDF document</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.New") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pdf</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Load PDF from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; $pdf; Test::PDF artwork) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Query page size for the first page:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.GetImportPageBounds"; $pdf; 1; "MediaBox") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$PageSize</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> split numbers, may need special handling with Math.TextToNumber to make sure it works if you use comma as decimal separator:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Math.TextToNumber"; GetValue($PageSize; 1)) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$left</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Math.TextToNumber"; GetValue($PageSize; 2)) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$top</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Math.TextToNumber"; GetValue($PageSize; 3)) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$right</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Math.TextToNumber"; GetValue($PageSize; 4)) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$bottom</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Abs($right - $left) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Width</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Abs($top- $bottom) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$height</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Calculate in centimeters</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>Round($Width * 2.54 / 72; 2) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Test" id="99999" name="Job dimension horizontal "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>Round($Height * 2.54 / 72; 2) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Test" id="99999" name="Job dimension vertical "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.Release"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> See also DynaPDF.RotateCoords DynaPDF.RotateTemplate DynaPDF.Save DynaPDF.SetImportFlags DynaPDF.SetImportFlags2 DynaPDF.SetMetadata DynaPDF.SetUseGlobalImpFiles DynaPDF.WebLink DynaPDF.WriteFTextEx Math.TextToNumber Release notes Version 14.5 Improved DynaPDF.GeneratePreview , DynaPDF.ReadImageResolution , DynaPDF.ReadImageFormat , DynaPDF.InsertImage , DynaPDF.AttachFileContainer , DynaPDF.AddOutputIntentEx , DynaPDF.CreateICCBasedColorSpaceContainer and DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer to return proper error if container is empty. examples.label: Query width and height of first page in PDF:
examples.text_code:
# Initialize DynaPDF if needed
If [
MBS
("
DynaPDF.IsInitialized
")  ≠  1 ]
Perform Script [ Specified: From list ; “Initialize DynaPDF” ; Parameter:    ]
End If
# Clear current PDF document
Set Variable [
$pdf
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.New
") ]
#
# Load PDF from container
#
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer";
$pdf
;
Test::PDF
artwork) ]
#
# Query page size for the first page:
#
Set Variable [
$PageSize
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.GetImportPageBounds
";
$pdf
; 1; "MediaBox") ]
#
# split numbers, may need special handling with
Math.TextToNumber
to make sure it works if you use comma as decimal separator:
#
Set Variable [
$left
; Value:
MBS
( "
Math.TextToNumber
"; GetValue(
$PageSize
; 1)) ]
Set Variable [
$top
; Value:
MBS
( "
Math.TextToNumber
"; GetValue(
$PageSize
; 2)) ]
Set Variable [
$right
; Value:
MBS
( "
Math.TextToNumber
"; GetValue(
$PageSize
; 3)) ]
Set Variable [
$bottom
; Value:
MBS
( "
Math.TextToNumber
"; GetValue(
$PageSize
; 4)) ]
#
Set Variable [
$Width
; Value: Abs(
$right
-
$left
) ]
Set Variable [
$height
; Value: Abs(
$top
-
$bottom
) ]
#
# Calculate in centimeters
#
Set Field [
Test::Job
dimension horizontal ; Round(
$Width
*
2.54
/ 72; 2) ]
Set Field [
Test::Job
dimension vertical ; Round(
$Height
*
2.54
/ 72; 2) ]
#
# Cleanup
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.Release
";
$pdf
) ]

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; $pdf; Test::PDF artwork) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Query page size for the first page:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.GetImportPageBounds"; $pdf; 1; "MediaBox") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$PageSize</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>

  </Step>

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Math.TextToNumber"; GetValue($PageSize; 1)) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$left</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Math.TextToNumber"; GetValue($PageSize; 2)) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>
