functions.name: DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile
functions.description:
Opens a PDF for importing from a file. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you have the PDF in a container, please use DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer function instead. Requires DynaPDF Lite license. If the function succeeds the return value is zero or the file handle if the flag UseProxy is set (a value greater or equal zero). If the function fails the return value is an error message. After opening the file, please use DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile or DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage to import one or more pages. Or just check metadata with e.g. DynaPDF.GetImportDocInfo and check page count and sizes with DynaPDF.GetImportPageBounds and DynaPDF.GetImportPageCount . See also OpenPDFFromFile function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Import selected pages from one PDF and write into a new one: Copy Text Copy XML # New PDF Set Variable [ $pdf ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.New ")] # Open existing PDF file (or use DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer for container) Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile"; $pdf ; $path )] # Query page count Set Variable [ $pageCount ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.GetImportPageCount "; $pdf )] # Import the pages you need Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage "; $pdf ; 1)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage "; $pdf ; 2)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage "; $pdf ; 3)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage "; $pdf ; $pagecount - 1)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage "; $pdf ; $pagecount - 2)] # Set output to this file path # MBS ( " DynaPDF.OpenOutputFile "; $pdf ; "/Users/cs/Desktop/output.pdf" ) # Write PDF Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Save "; $pdf )] # release memory Set Variable [ $r1 ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Release "; $pdf )] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> New PDF</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.New")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pdf</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Open existing PDF file (or use DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer for container)</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile"; $pdf; $path )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Query page count</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.GetImportPageCount"; $pdf )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pageCount</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Import the pages you need</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage"; $pdf; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage"; $pdf; 2)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage"; $pdf; 3)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage"; $pdf; $pagecount - 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage"; $pdf; $pagecount - 2)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Set output to this file path</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> MBS( "DynaPDF.OpenOutputFile"; $pdf; "/Users/cs/Desktop/output.pdf" )</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Write PDF</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.Save"; $pdf)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> release memory</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.Release"; $pdf)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r1</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Import a single page: Copy Text Copy XML Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetImportFlags "; $pdf ; "ImportAll ImportAsPage")] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile; $pdf ; "C:\test.pdf")] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage "; $pdf ; 1)] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Load PDF and extract text of page 2: Copy Text Copy XML # initialize DynaPDF before running this: Set Variable [ $pdf ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.New ")] # load PDF from container Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile"; $pdf ; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.pdf")] # import all pages Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile "; $pdf )] # extra text of page 2 Set Field [ Test::PageText ; MBS (" DynaPDF.ExtractPageText "; $pdf ; 2)] #Cleanup Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Release "; $pdf )] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> initialize DynaPDF before running this:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.New")</Calculation>
    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>

</fmxmlsnippet> Read in a file, add text and write back: Copy Text Copy XML # Start new PDF Set Variable [ $pdf ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.New ") ] # # import existing file Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile"; $pdf ; "/Users/cs/Temp/Installation.pdf") ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile "; $pdf ; 1) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.CloseImportFile "; $pdf ) ] # # now edit a page Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.EditPage "; $pdf ; 1) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetFont "; $pdf ; "Helvetica"; 0; 20) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.WriteText "; $pdf ; 100; 100; "Hello World") ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.EndPage "; $pdf ) ] # # save back to same file Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.OpenOutputFile "; $pdf ; "/Users/cs/Temp/Installation.pdf") ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Save "; $pdf ) ] # # cleanup Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Release "; $pdf ) ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

    <Repetition>

examples.label: Import selected pages from one PDF and write into a new one:
examples.text_code:
# New PDF
Set Variable [
$pdf
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.New
")]
# Open existing PDF file (or use
DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer
for container)
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromFile";
$pdf
;
$path
)]
# Query page count
Set Variable [
$pageCount
; Value:
MBS
( "
DynaPDF.GetImportPageCount
";
$pdf
)]
# Import the pages you need
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage
";
$pdf
; 1)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage
";
$pdf
; 2)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage
";
$pdf
; 3)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage
";
$pdf
;
$pagecount
- 1)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage
";
$pdf
;
$pagecount
- 2)]
# Set output to this file path
#

( "
DynaPDF.OpenOutputFile
";
$pdf
; "/Users/cs/Desktop/output.pdf" )
# Write PDF

DynaPDF.Save
";

    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>

    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

functions.name: DynaPDF.OpenTag
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.OpenTag"; PDF; Tag { ; Lang; AltText; Expansion; BoxX1; BoxY1; BoxX2; BoxY2 } )
functions.description: Opens the specified tag so that contents can be written into it. When the corresponding contents was drawn or output the tag must be closed with DynaPDF.CloseTag . The supported tags have the same meaning as the corresponding HTML tags. Special tags:

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: Tag
parameters.description: The tag name to open. Possible tags: Art, Artifact, Annot, BibEntry, BlockQuote, Caption, Code, Div, Document, Figure, Form, Formula, H, H1, H2, H3, H4, H5, H6, Index, Link, List, ListElem, ListText, Note, P, Part, Quote, Reference, Section, Span, Table, TableDataCell, TableHeader, TableRow, TOC, TOCEntry.

parameters.name: Lang
parameters.description: RFC 3066 laguage identifier.

parameters.name: AltText
parameters.description: Alternate text.

parameters.name: Expansion
parameters.description: Expansion of abbrevations or acronyms.

parameters.name: BoxX1
parameters.description: The top left x coordiante for the tag.

parameters.name: BoxY1
parameters.description: The top top y coordiante for the tag.

parameters.name: BoxX2
parameters.description: The top right x coordiante for the tag.

parameters.name: BoxY2
parameters.description: The top bottom y coordiante for the tag.

functions.name: DynaPDF.OpenTagEx
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.OpenTagEx"; PDF; Tag; Lang; AltText; Expansion; Attributes )
functions.description: Opens a new tag. Opens a tag in the very same way as DynaPDF.OpenTag but supports additional parameters which can be set in a JSON (Javascript Object Notation) like format. The optional parameter Attributes is a JSON like string. The JSON parser in DynaPDF supports a few extensions to make the definition of additional attributes as easy as possible. See also OpenTagEx function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.OpenTag Release notes Version 13.3 Added DynaPDF.AddDPartNode , DynaPDF.CreateDPartRoot , DynaPDF.DeleteWatermark , DynaPDF.GetLastTextOffset , DynaPDF.HaveDPartRoot , DynaPDF.MarkTemplateAsWatermark and DynaPDF.OpenTagEx functions. parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: Tag
parameters.description: The tag name to open. Possible tags: Art, Artifact, Annot, BibEntry, BlockQuote, Caption, Code, Div, Document, Figure, Form, Formula, H, H1, H2, H3, H4, H5, H6, Index, Link, List, ListElem, ListText, Note, P, Part, Quote, Reference, Section, Span, Table, TableDataCell, TableHeader, TableRow, TOC, TOCEntry.

parameters.name: Lang
parameters.description: RFC 3066 laguage identifier.

parameters.name: AltText
parameters.description: Alternate text.

parameters.name: Expansion
parameters.description: Expansion of abbrevations or acronyms.

parameters.name: Attributes
parameters.description: The attributes as JSON.

functions.name: DynaPDF.Optimize
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.Optimize"; PDF { ; Flags; Min1BitRes; MinGrayRes; MinColorRes; Res1BitImages; ResGrayImages; ResColorImages; Filter1Bit; FilterGray; FilterColor; JPEGQuality; JP2KQuality; MinLineWidth } )
functions.description: Optimizes PDF. This repairs also a lot of errors in the PDF and can shrink the PDF size.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: The flags to use. Can be a number with values from dynapdf documentation, see table on this page.

parameters.name: Min1BitRes
parameters.description: Minimum resolution before scaling for one bit images. Default is 100. Can be zero to disable.

parameters.name: MinGrayRes
parameters.description: Minimum resolution before scaling for gray images. Default is 100. Can be zero to disable.

parameters.name: MinColorRes
parameters.description: Minimum resolution before scaling for color images. Default is 100. Can be zero to disable.

parameters.name: Res1BitImages
parameters.description: Resolution for 1 bit black & white images. Default is 150.

parameters.name: ResGrayImages
parameters.description: Resolution for gray images. Default is 150.

parameters.name: ResColorImages
parameters.description: Resolution for images with more than one color channel. Default is 150.

parameters.name: Filter1Bit
parameters.description: Filter for black & white images. Can be Flate, JPEG, CCITT3, CCITT4, LZW, FlateBW or JP2K. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: FilterGray
parameters.description: Filter for gray images. Can be Flate, JPEG, CCITT3, CCITT4, LZW, FlateBW or JP2K. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: FilterColor
parameters.description: Filter for multi-channel images. Can be Flate, JPEG, CCITT3, CCITT4, LZW, FlateBW or JP2K. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: JPEGQuality
parameters.description: JPEG quality. Default is 80.

parameters.name: JP2KQuality
parameters.description: JPEG 2000 quality Default is 80.

parameters.name: MinLineWidth
parameters.description: Minimum line width for lines. Zero means no hair line removal.

examples.label: Custom function for easy optimization:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ([ 
// Custom Function OptimizePDF(InputPDF)
//
// Optimizes PDF and returns either new one or old one in case of error.

PDF = MBS("DynaPDF.New");
OpenResult = MBS("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; PDF; InputPDF);
OpenError = MBS("IsError");
ImportResult = MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile"; PDF);
ImportError = MBS("IsError");
Options = "FlattenLayers DeleteInvPaths ScaleImages NewLinkNames DeletePrivateData IgnoreZeroLineWidth DeleteAlternateImages DeleteThumbnails";
OptimizeResult = MBS("DynaPDF.Optimize"; PDF; Options; 50; 50; 50; 150; 150; 150; "JPEG"; "JPEG"; "JPEG");
OptimizeError = MBS("IsError");
OutputPDF = MBS("DynaPDF.Save"; PDF; GetAsText(InputPDF));
SaveError = MBS("IsError");
ReleaseError = MBS("DynaPDF.Release"; PDF);

Result = If( 
  // all okay?
  OpenError = 0 and ImportError = 0 and OptimizeError = 0 and SaveError = 0 and MBS( "Container.GetSize"; OutputPDF; "PDF " ) > 0; 
  // than return new PDF
  OutputPDF;
  // return old PDF
  InputPDF)
 
];
result )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DynaPDF.PageLink
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.PageLink"; PDF; PosX; PosY; Width; Height; DestPage )
functions.description:
Adds a page link to the current open page. The parameter DestPage specifies the destination page which should be opened (the page number). The function does not check whether the destination page exists; it can be created later. If the destination page does not exist when the document is closed it will be set to the first page. If the coordinate system is bottom-up the point PosX, PosY defines the lower left corner of the bounding rectangle. If the coordinate system is top-down it defines the upper left corner. The border of the link annotation is drawn by using the current line width, stroke color and line dash pattern. If the link should appear without a border set the line width to zero beforehand (with DynaPDF.SetLineWidth ). When clicking on a link annotation the rectangle is highlighted, that is a simple visual effect. Several highlight modes are supported, see DynaPDF.SetLinkHighlightMode for further information. The destination page is always opened at the top corner. More precise destinations can be created with the function DynaPDF.PageLinkEx . Page links can also be used to execute an action. The destination page is ignored in the latter case, the annotation works then like a button. If the function succeeds the return value is the annotation handle, a value greater or equal zero. If the function fails the return value is an error message. Use DynaPDF.WebLink for web links, DynaPDF.FileLink for file links, DynaPDF.PageLink or DynaPDF.PageLinkEx for page links and DynaPDF.FindText can help to find the location where to place the link. See also PageLink function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Add various links to a page: Copy Text Copy XML # import page Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer "; $pdf ; Add Page Numbers::Input )] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile "; $pdf ; 1)] # edit first page Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.EditPage "; $pdf ; 1)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.SetFont "; $pdf ; "Helvetica"; 0; 20)] #Add web link Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.WriteFTextEx "; $PDF ; 100; 100; 150; 20; "left"; "Go to MBS Blog" )] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.WebLink "; $PDF ; 100; 80; 150; 20; "https://www.mbsplugins.de/filemaker" )] #Add page link Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.WriteFTextEx "; $PDF ; 100; 150; 150; 20; "left"; "Jump to page 3" )] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( "DynaPDF.PageLink"; $PDF ; 100; 130; 150; 20; 3)] #Add page link with more options Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.WriteFTextEx "; $PDF ; 100; 200; 200; 20; "left"; "Jump to Letter D Zoomed" )] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.PageLinkEx "; $PDF ; 100; 180; 200; 20; "FitRect"; 4; 60; 590; 135; 160)] # Close page Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.EndPage "; $pdf )] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

functions.name: DynaPDF.PageLinkEx
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.PageLinkEx"; PDF; PosX; PosY; Width; Height; DestinationType; DestPage { ; a; b; c; d } )
functions.description: Adds a page link to the current open page. The parameter DestPage specifies the destination page which should be opened (the page number). The function does not check whether the destination page exists; it can be created later. If the destination page is not created before the document is closed, it will be set to the first page of the document. If the coordinate system is bottom-up the point PosX, PosY defines the lower left corner of the bounding rectangle. If the coordinate system is top-down it defines the upper left corner. The border of the link annotation is drawn by using the current line width, stroke color and line dash pattern. If the link should appear without a border set the line width to zero beforehand (with DynaPDF.SetLineWidth ). When clicking on a link annotation the rectangle is highlighted, that is a simple visual effect. Several highlight modes are supported, see DynaPDF.SetLinkHighlightMode for further information. The destination created by this annotation allows jumping to a specific position in a document instead of simply opening the page such as DynaPDF.PageLink does.

parameters.description: X-Coordinate of bounding rectangle

parameters.description: Y-Coordinate of bounding rectangle

parameters.description: Width of bounding rectangle

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: Height of bounding rectangle

parameters.description: The destination type.

parameters.name: DestPage
parameters.description: The destination page number.

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: First parameter for destination type.

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: Second parameter for destination type.

parameters.name: c
parameters.description: Third parameter for destination type.

parameters.name: d
parameters.description: Forth parameter for destination type.

functions.name: DynaPDF.PageStatistics
functions.description: Queries the page statistics. The statistics are returned as one value per line in a multi line text. The order of the values is: Number of patterns used. Number of templates Number of bezier curves Number of path clipping Number of path closing Number of shading drawings Number of images Number of lines Number of rectangles Number of texts Length of all texts together Number of layers Number of font loadings This function uses the parser interface in DynaPDF and needs a Pro license. If you have an open page, we close it automatically for you before doing the import.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: PageIndex
parameters.description: The page to check. Range is from one to DynaPDF.GetPageCount .

</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DynaPDF.ParseContent
functions.description:
Runs the content parser. Returns a list of text representations of the various PDF commands founds. Output shows entries like this: SaveGraphicState Rectangle X: 0.000000 Y: 0.000000 W: 842.000000 H: 595.000000 ClipPath EvenOdd: 0 PathFillMode: 10 SetFillColor IColorSpace: 0x843e34d0 ExtColorSpace: 6 NumComps: 1 Color[0]: 1.000000 MulMatrix Matrix: 1.000000 0.000000 0.000000 -1.000000 0.000000 595.000000 Rectangle X: 19.842520 Y: 19.637820 W: 240.944900 H: 555.690600 SetFont FontType: 1 Embedded: 1 FontName: Cochin Style: 419430400, FontSize: 1.000000, Font: 0x86131e00 ShowTextArray Count: 1 Width: 1.500000 Matrix: 13.000000 0.000000 0.000000 -13.000000 63.150010 237.000000 SourceAdvance[0]: 0.000000 SourceLength[0]: 3 SourceText[0]: SourceAdvance[0]: 0.000000 SourceLength[0]: 3 SourceText[0]: +49 KerningAdvance[0]: 0.000000 KerningLength[0]: 3 KerningWidth[0]: 1.500000 KerningText[0]: +49 This function uses the parser interface in DynaPDF and needs a Pro license. If you have an open page, we close it automatically for you before doing the import. See also ParseContent function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Parse content of page 6: Copy Text Copy XML MBS ( "DynaPDF.ParseContent"; $pdf ; 6  ) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.ParseContent"; $pdf; 6  )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> See also DynaPDF.GetContent DynaPDF.GetPageCount DynaPDF.Initialize DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.Parser.Content DynaPDF.SetContent DynaPDF.SetRenderingIntent Release notes Version 13.2 Fixed DynaPDF.ParseContent to not complain about open page. Version 10.2 Fixed crash in DynaPDF.ParseContent function. Blog Entries MBS FileMaker Plugin, version 13.2pr1 MBS FileMaker Plugin, version 10.2pr1 MBS Filemaker Plugin, version 4.0pr1 This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 25th September 2024 DynaPDF.PageStatistics - DynaPDF.Parser.ChangeAltFont 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: PageIndex
parameters.description: The page to check. Range is from one to DynaPDF.GetPageCount .

examples.label: Parse content of page 6:

( "DynaPDF.ParseContent";
$pdf
; 6  )

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DynaPDF.Parser.ChangeAltFont
functions.description: Changes alternative font to use. Changes the active alternate font that is used by DynaPDF.Parser.ReplaceSelText when the original font is not available. The font handle must be a handle that was returned by DynaPDF.SetFont , DynaPDF.SetFontEx , DynaPDF.SetCIDFont, or DynaPDF.Parser.SetAltFont .

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference.

parameters.name: FontHandle
parameters.description: The font handle.

# Initialize DynaPDF if needed

("

# Clear current PDF document
Set Variable [
$pdf
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.New
") ]
# Load PDF from container
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:

("
DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer
";
$pdf
;
DynaPDF Replace Text::InputPDF
) ]
# import a page
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage
";
$pdf
; 1) ]

Set Variable [

; Value: 0 ]

; Value:

DynaPDF.Parser.Create

;
$OptimizeFlags

";
$pdf

functions.name: DynaPDF.Parser.Create
functions.description: Creates the parser object. The function creates a parser context that can be used to edit and extract text, or do delete arbitrary operators of a page. The content parser is used by DynaPDF.Optimize too. Therefore, the same flags and optimization parameters are supported. Please have a look at DynaPDF.Optimize for a description of the available flags and parameters. The parameter OptimizeParams can be set to nil and OptimizeFlags to "Default" if nothing special should be achieved.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: The flags to use. Can be a number with values from dynapdf documentation, see table on this page.

examples.label: Initialize parser:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$OptimizeFlags
; Value: 0 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "DynaPDF.Parser.Create";
$pdf
;
$OptimizeFlags
) ]
examples.xml_code:

      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$OptimizeFlags</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.Parser.Create"; $pdf; $OptimizeFlags ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

parameters.name: Index

functions.name: DynaPDF.Parser.DeleteAllImages
functions.description: Delete all images. This deletes inline and regular image drawing commands from the page. You need to call DynaPDF.Parser.WriteToPage later to save changes back to page.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: DeleteInlines
parameters.description: Whether to delete inline images. Pass a boolean as 1 to delete or 0 to not delete inlines. Defaults to 1.

functions.name: DynaPDF.Parser.DeleteImage
functions.description: Deletes one image from the page. We look for the first image on the page matching the given handle number and remove it. If the same image is shown multiple times on the page, it appears multiple time in the array returned by ListImages and you need to call DeleteImage multiple times. You need to call DynaPDF.Parser.WriteToPage later to save changes back to page.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: ImageHandle
parameters.description: The image handle for the image to remove. From 0 to DynaPDF.GetImageCount -1.

parameters.name: Nth
parameters.description: The offset of the image in the list. If the same picture is shown multiple times on the page, you can put a number here to address is. Default is 0.

functions.name: DynaPDF.Parser.DeleteText
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.Parser.DeleteText"; PDF; Left; Bottom; Right; Top )
functions.description: Deletes text in the area. The function deletes every glyph or character that touches or lies inside the rectangle Area. Area must be defined as if the page would be viewed in a PDF viewer. That means in bottom up coordinates and the orientation must be considered (see DynaPDF.GetOrientation ). The width and height of a page must be calculated from the crop box if set, or from the media box otherwise (see DynaPDF.GetPageBBox ). Note also that the width and height must be exchanged if the orientation is 90, -90, 270, or -270 degrees. Note that this function deletes text only. Text can also occur in form of images or vector graphics. There are no functions yet to identify and delete text in such objects. If the function succeeds the return value is true. If the function fails the return value is false.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference.

parameters.name: Left
parameters.description: The left coordinate of the rectangle.

parameters.name: Bottom
parameters.description: The bottom coordinate of the page.

parameters.name: Right
parameters.description: The right coordinate of the page.

parameters.name: Top
parameters.description: The top coordinate of the page.

examples.label: Delete a rectangle in text:
examples.text_code:
# initialize parser
Set Variable [
$OptimizeFlags
; Value: 0 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
DynaPDF.Parser.Create
";
$pdf
;
$OptimizeFlags
) ]
#
# Now parse a page
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.Parser.ParsePage
";
$pdf
; 1; "EnableTextSelection") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to parse page" ;
$r
]
Else
# Define the rectangle
Set Variable [
$areaLeft
; Value: 200 ]
Set Variable [
$areaWidth
; Value: 200 ]
Set Variable [
$areaTop
; Value: 200 ]
Set Variable [
$areaHeight
; Value: 200 ]
#
# we need right and bottom. And since coordinates are bottom-up, we bottom is the smaller y value
Set Variable [
$areaRight
; Value:
$areaLeft
+
$areaWidth
]
Set Variable [
$areaBottom
; Value:
$areaTop
]
Set Variable [
$areaTop
; Value:
$areaTop
+
$areaHeight
]
#
# now delete text in that area
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "DynaPDF.Parser.DeleteText";
$pdf
;
$areaTop
;
$areaBottom
;
$areaLeft
;
$areaRight
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to parse page" ;
$r
]
Else
# Save changes back
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
DynaPDF.Parser.WriteToPage
";
$pdf
;
$OptimizeFlags
) ]
End If
End If

      <Calculation>200 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$areaWidth</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>200 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$areaTop</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>200 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$areaHeight</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> we need right and bottom. And since coordinates are bottom-up, we bottom is the smaller y value</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$areaLeft + $areaWidth </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$areaRight</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$areaTop </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$areaBottom</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$areaTop + $areaHeight </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$areaTop</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now delete text in that area</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.Parser.DeleteText"; $pdf; $areaTop; $areaBottom; $areaLeft; $areaRight) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to parse page" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">

      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Save changes back</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.Parser.WriteToPage"; $pdf; $OptimizeFlags) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DynaPDF.Parser.ExtractText
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.Parser.ExtractText"; PDF; Flags { ; Left; Bottom; Right; Top } )
functions.description: Extracts text from parser. The function extracts the text of a page with the same algorithm that DynaPDF.FindText uses to find text on a page. In order to get exactly the same result the flag "SortTextX" must be set. The function DynaPDF.ExtractText of the PDF instance calls in fact this function internally. The optional parameter Area defined using left, bottom, right and top can be set to restrict text extraction to that rectangle. The rectangle must be defined as if the page would be viewed in a PDF viewer. That means in bottom up coordinates and the orientation must be considered. The page coordinate system is de-rotated before text extraction starts since this produces better results. The width and height must be calculated from the crop box if set, or from the media box otherwise. Note also that the width and height must be exchanged if the orientation is 90, -90, 270, or -270 degrees.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: The flags for text extraction. Can include Default, SortTextX, SortTextY, SortTextXY, DeleteOverlappingText and/or NoHeuristic. Usually you may want to use SortTextX here. The flag MediaBox limits text extraction to the media box. The flag CropBox uses the crop box (if missing media box) for the rectangle.

parameters.name: Left
parameters.description: The left coordinate of the rectangle.

parameters.name: Bottom
parameters.description: The bottom coordinate of the page.

parameters.name: Right
parameters.description: The right coordinate of the page.

parameters.name: Top
parameters.description: The top coordinate of the page.

examples.label: Extract text of a page
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$page
; Value: 1 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.Parser.ParsePage
";
$pdf
;
$page
; "EnableTextSelection") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to parse page" ;
$r
]
Else
Set Variable [
$$text
; Value:
MBS
("DynaPDF.Parser.ExtractText";
$pdf
;
$page
; "SortTextX NoHeuristic CropBox") ]
End If

functions.name: DynaPDF.Parser.FindText
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.Parser.FindText"; PDF; Text; SearchType; ContinueSearch { ; Left; Bottom; Right; Top } )
functions.description: Finds text using the parser. Searches for text and stores the result so that further editing actions can be applied. Area must be defined as if the page would be viewed in a PDF viewer. That means in bottom up coordinates and the orientation must be considered (see DynaPDF.GetOrientation ). The width and height of a page must be calculated from the crop box if set, or from the media box otherwise (see DynaPDF.GetBBox ). Note also that the width and height must be exchanged if the orientation is 90, -90, 270, or -270 degrees. The page coordinate system is de-rotated since this produces better results and it is much easier to find the location of text in rotated pages. DynaPDF.Parser.FindText is usually called inside a loop until no more occurrences of the search string can be found. In the first call, continueSearch must be false. That means start at the beginning. The continue searches with continueSearch = true. Available search types:

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to find.

parameters.name: SearchType
parameters.description: The search type. Can be a combination of Default, MatchAlways, SearchAsIs, WholeWord, CaseInSensitive or CaseSensitive.

parameters.name: ContinueSearch
parameters.description: Whether to start fresh (False) or continue search (true).

parameters.name: Left
parameters.description: The left coordinate of the rectangle.

parameters.name: Bottom
parameters.description: The bottom coordinate of the page.

parameters.name: Right
parameters.description: The right coordinate of the page.

parameters.name: Top
parameters.description: The top coordinate of the page.

examples.label: Perform find and replace:
examples.text_code:
# initialize parser
Set Variable [
$OptimizeFlags
; Value: 0 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
DynaPDF.Parser.Create
";
$pdf
;
$OptimizeFlags
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
DynaPDF.Parser.SetAltFont
";
$pdf
; "Helvetica"; 0; 12) ]
#
# Now parse a page
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.Parser.ParsePage
";
$pdf
; 1; "EnableTextSelection") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to parse page" ;
$r
]
Else
# Run a find
Set Variable [
$continueFind
; Value: 0 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "DynaPDF.Parser.FindText";
$pdf
;
DynaPDF Replace Text::SearchText
; "CaseInsensitive";
$continueFind
) ]
If [
$r
= 1 ]
Loop [ Flush: Always ]
# Do the replace
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
DynaPDF.Parser.ReplaceSelText
";
$pdf
;
DynaPDF Replace Text::ReplaceText
) ]
#
# Continue search
Set Variable [
$continueFind
; Value: 1 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "DynaPDF.Parser.FindText";
$pdf
;
DynaPDF Replace Text::SearchText
; "CaseInsensitive";
$continueFind
) ]
Exit Loop If [
$r
≠ 1 ]
End Loop
#
# Save changes back
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
DynaPDF.Parser.WriteToPage
";
$pdf
;
$OptimizeFlags
) ]
End If
End If

functions.name: DynaPDF.Parser.Line
functions.description: Queries the index of the found line. Can be called after DynaPDF.Parser.FindText function found something.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference.

functions.name: DynaPDF.Parser.ListImages
functions.description: Lists all images on the page. This function returns a list of image handle numbers. Does not include inline images. You can use DynaPDF.GetImage with the given image handles to query information.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

functions.name: DynaPDF.Parser.ParsePage
functions.description: Parses a page. The function parses a page and stores the page contents in a objects internally. Once a page was parsed various functions can be called, e.g. to extract the text of a page, to find and replace text, or to delete arbitrary operators. The page that should be parsed should be closed, that means it should not be opened for editing beforehand with DynaPDF.EditPage or DynaPDF.AppendPage . The function can parse an open page too but this can lead to errors and is not recommended. If the function succeeds the return value is true. If the function fails the return value is false.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference.

parameters.name: PageNum
parameters.description: The page number.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: The content parser flags.

functions.name: DynaPDF.Parser.ReplaceSelText
functions.description: Replaces text on the page. The function replaces or deletes the text that was found by DynaPDF.Parser.FindText . Coordinates of surrounding text are not changed, this means that the new text can overlap surrounding text if the width of the new text is larger than the original text. So, text replacement has its limitations since at some point text must might be new formatted or re-aligned. However, replacing placeholders with text is usually no problem as long as there is enough room for the new text. Note that placeholders should not contain space characters since spaces are often not stored in PDF files and this can lead to issues finding the text. Font substitution Text replacement depends on the availability of the fonts which are used in a PDF file. If the original font is not available the function loads an alternate font that matches the characteristics of the original font as close as possible. However, font substitution is not perfect and a substituted font looks sometimes more different as expected. To improve text replacement it is possible to set one or more alternate fonts which should be used if the original font cannot be found on the system. Alternate fonts can be loaded with DynaPDF.Parser.SetAltFont . However, a font loaded by DynaPDF.SetFont , DynaPDF.SetFontEx or DynaPDF.SetCIDFont works too. In order to activate a font loaded by a regular font loading function call DynaPDF.Parser.ChangeAltFont . It is possible to load more than one alternate font but only the active font will be used when replacing text. If more than one font must be loaded, store the handle returned by DynaPDF.Parser.SetAltFont and change the font with DynaPDF.Parser.ChangeAltFont whenever needed.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The new text. Can be empty to replace text with nothing. You can’t use line breaks here.

    <Calculation> $r ≠ 1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Save changes back</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.Parser.WriteToPage"; $pdf; $OptimizeFlags) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DynaPDF.Parser.Reset
functions.description: Resets the parser.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference.

functions.name: DynaPDF.Parser.SelectionBBox
functions.description: Computes the bounding box of the current selection. Use after DynaPDF.Parser.FindText to query selection location on page. The bounding box is calculated in top down coordinates! This differs in comparison to almost all other functions which return PDF coordinates. In order to place objects on this position it is usually best to enable visible coordinates with DynaPDF.SetUseVisibleCoords and to rotate the coordinate system with DynaPDF.SetOrientationEx according to the page orientation after the page was opened for editing. Returns a list of values with coordinates, x1/y1 and x2/y2.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference.

functions.result: Returns list of values or error.

functions.name: DynaPDF.Parser.SelectionText
functions.description: Retrieves the text and text length of the current selection or found text. The function is useful if FindText() was called with the search type MatchAlways. In this case, and only in this case, it is not known which text FindText() has found.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference.

functions.name: DynaPDF.Parser.SetAltFont
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.Parser.SetAltFont"; PDF; Name { ; Style; Size; Embed; CodePage } )
functions.description: Sets the alternative font. Sets the font as alternate font that is used by DynaPDF.Parser.ReplaceSelText if the original font is not available. Although the parameter Size must be greater zero, the value is in fact not used when replacing text. The parameter is reserved for future use. Please note that the replacement text must be defined in the code page with which the font was loaded. To effiently change the font whenever needed, call DynaPDF.Parser.ChangeAltFont . It is also possible to call DynaPDF.Parser.SetAltFont but this would require more processing time. If the function succeeds the return value is the font handle, a value greater of equal zero. If the function fails the return value is 0.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: Font name for new font to set or empty text to deactivate current font.

parameters.name: Style
parameters.description: Font style value. Can be a combination of values 1 for italic, 2 for bold, 4 for underlined and 8 for striked. With version 12.0 or newer, this can be passed as text.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Font size in points.

parameters.name: Embed
parameters.description: Whether to embed font in PDF. Can be 1 to embed or 0 to not embed. Default is 1.

examples.label: Set alternative font:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "DynaPDF.Parser.SetAltFont";
$pdf
; "Helvetica"; 0; 12) ]

functions.name: DynaPDF.Parser.TextMatrix
functions.description: Text matrix pre-calculated in user space. Can be called after FindText function found something. This defines where the current text is on the page. Returns list with 6 numbers describing the matrix: a, b, c, d, x, y. Depending on the locale of your FileMaker, they use command or dot, so they should just work with GetAsNumber() when reading.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference.

functions.name: DynaPDF.Parser.WriteToPage
functions.description: Writes changes back to the page. Writes the objects, that was created by DynaPDF.Parser.ParsePage , back to the page. The flags and optional parameters were taken from DynaPDF.Optimize because DynaPDF.Optimize uses the very same parser to optimize pages. Please have a look at this function to determine which flags and parameters are available. Unchanged pages can be left unchanged or written back to the page. This is up to you. If DynaPDF.Parser.WriteToPage is called then the content stream will be optimized. If nothing special should be achieved set Flags to "Default". Needs Pro license for DynaPDF.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: The flags to use. Can be a number with values from dynapdf documentation, see table on this page.

functions.name: DynaPDF.PlaceSigFieldValidateIcon
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.PlaceSigFieldValidateIcon"; PDF; SigField; PosX; PosY; Width; Height )
functions.description: Places the validation icon within a signature field to the wished position. By default, the validation icon is scaled to the width or height of the signature field and it is horizontally and vertically centered. When creating a user defined appearance for a signature field it is sometimes required to place the validation icon on another position within the signature field so that no important contents will be overdrawn. The unscaled size of the validation icon is 100 x 100 units. It can be scaled to every size you want but it is usually best to preserve the aspect ratio. The icon should normally be placed fully inside the signature field. If the validation icon should not be shown then place it outside the signature field. See also DynaPDF.CreateSigFieldAP . Please note that the position of the validation icon is no property that can be stored in the PDF file. The position and size that you set with this function can only be considered if the file will be signed later with DynaPDF (see DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFile for further information). See also PlaceSigFieldValidateIcon function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.CreateSigField DynaPDF.CreateSigFieldAP DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.Save DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFile Release notes Version 10.2 Added DynaPDF.ApplyShading , DynaPDF.CreateAxialShading , DynaPDF.CreateRadialShading and DynaPDF.PlaceSigFieldValidateIcon functions. Example Databases DynaPDF/Signature Appearance Blog Entries MBS FileMaker Plugin, version 10.2pr5 Digitally signing PDF with DynaPDF and custom signature appearance This function checks for a license. Created 20th April 2020 , last changed 20th April 2020 DynaPDF.Parser.WriteToPage - DynaPDF.PlaceTemplate 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: DynaPDF.PlaceTemplate
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.PlaceTemplate"; PDF; TemplateHandle; X; Y; W; H )
functions.description:
The function places a template on a page, another open template, or pattern. The parameter TemplateHandle must be a valid template handle that was returned by DynaPDF.BeginTemplate or DynaPDF.ImportPageAsTemplate . Templates can be used multiple times on different pages or positions and with different sizes. Unlike images, a template can be scaled without losing quality as far as the template contains vector graphics and text objects only. The calculation of the width and height is the same as for images: If Width or Height is -1 the function uses the mirrored original width or height from the template. If Width or Height is 0, the missing value is calculated in relation to the given value of Height or Width to preserve the template's aspect ratio. The resulting output is a template with exact proportions relative to its original size. If Width and Height are 0, the original size is used (same effect as -1 but the template is not mirrored). A negative value of Width or Height mirrors the template on the x- or y-axis. Imported pages can contain non-normalized bounding boxes and the page can be rotated. The coordinate origin, crop box, and rotation angle of a page must be considered when placing a template on a page because templates do not support features like a crop box or individual orientation angles. The required calculations to find the correct coordinate origin and to de- rotate a template are relatively complex. Therefore, DynaPDF provides the function DynaPDF.PlaceTemplateEx which considers the, coordinate origin, orientation angle, and crop box automatically. DynaPDF.PlaceTemplateEx greatly simplifies the handling of templates. If possible, this function should be used when working with imported pages. Remarks: A template is invisible as long it was not placed on a page, template, or pattern. Templates should not be used inside of patterns because this requires usually too much rendering time. See also PlaceTemplate function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Place page rotated: Copy Text Copy XML Set Variable [ $pdf ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.New " ) ] # import pdf Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer " ; $pdf ; Render Page::Input PDF ) ] Set Variable [ $template ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.ImportPageAsTemplate "; $PDF ; 1 ) ] # check how big our template is Set Variable [ $bOriginal ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.GetTemplWidth " ; $pdf ; $template ) ] Set Variable [ $hOriginal ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.GetTemplHeight " ; $pdf ; $template ) ] # now edit page Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.EditPage "; $pdf ; 1 ) ] # Rotation metadata to 0° Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetOrientation "; $pdf ; 0 ) ] # chage page size to size of template Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.SetPageWidth "; $pdf ; $hOriginal ) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.SetPageHeight "; $pdf ; $bOriginal ) ] # rotate page in center by 90° counter clockwise Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.TranslateCoords "; $pdf ; MBS (" DynaPDF.GetPageWidth "; $pdf )/2; MBS ( " DynaPDF.GetPageHeight "; $pdf )/2) & MBS (" DynaPDF.RotateCoords "; $pdf ; -90; 0; 0) & MBS (" DynaPDF.TranslateCoords "; $pdf ; - MBS ( " DynaPDF.GetPageHeight "; $pdf )/2; - MBS ("DynaPDF.GetP… ] # now place template and close page Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( "DynaPDF.PlaceTemplate" ; $pdf ; $template ; 0 ; 0 ; $bOriginal ; $hOriginal ) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.EndPage " ; $pdf ) ] # save Set Field [ Render Page::Output Image ; MBS ( " DynaPDF.Save " ; $pdf ; "Quadrat.pdf" ) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.Release " ; $pdf ) ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pdf</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

    <Value>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>

    </Repetition>

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>

    </Value>

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS ("DynaPDF.SetOrientation"; $pdf; 0 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> chage page size to size of template</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS ( "DynaPDF.SetPageWidth"; $pdf ; $hOriginal ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS ( "DynaPDF.SetPageHeight"; $pdf ; $bOriginal ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> rotate page in center by 90° counter clockwise</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.TranslateCoords"; $pdf; MBS("DynaPDF.GetPageWidth";$pdf)/2; MBS( "DynaPDF.GetPageHeight";$pdf)/2) & MBS("DynaPDF.RotateCoords"; $pdf; -90; 0; 0) & MBS("DynaPDF.TranslateCoords"; $pdf; -MBS( "DynaPDF.GetPageHeight";$pdf)/2; -MBS("DynaPDF.GetP… </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now place template and close page</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS ( "DynaPDF.PlaceTemplate" ; $pdf ; $template ; 0 ; 0 ; $bOriginal ; $hOriginal ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS ( "DynaPDF.EndPage" ; $pdf ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> save</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS ( "DynaPDF.Save" ; $pdf ; "Quadrat.pdf" ) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Render Page" id="99999" name="Output Image "/>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> See also DynaPDF.BeginTemplate DynaPDF.DeleteTemplate DynaPDF.DeleteTemplateEx DynaPDF.EditTemplate DynaPDF.EditTemplate2 DynaPDF.RotateTemplate DynaPDF.Save DynaPDF.SetOrientation DynaPDF.SetPageCoords DynaPDF.TranslateCoords Blog Entries Things you can do with DynaPDF Normalize PDF orientation with MBS Plugins and DynaPDF FileMaker Magazin Ausgabe 5/2021, Seite 29 This function checks for a license. functions.name: DynaPDF.PlaceTemplateEx
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.PlaceTemplateEx"; PDF; TemplateHandle; X; Y; W; H )
functions.description: The function places a template on a page, another open template, or pattern. The parameter TemplateHandle must be a valid template handle that was returned by DynaPDF.BeginTemplate , DynaPDF.ImportPageAsTemplate . The function was specifically designed to work with templates which were created from imported pages. A PDF page is converted to a template if it was imported with DynaPDF.ImportPageAsTemplate outside of an open page. The calculation of the width and height is the same as for images: If Width or Height is -1 the function uses the mirrored original width or height from the template. If Width or Height is 0, the missing value is calculated in relation to the given value of Height or Width to preserve the template's aspect ratio. The resulting output is a template with exact proportions relative to its original size. If Width and Height are 0, the original size is used (same effect as -1 but the template is not mirrored). A negative value of Width or Height mirrors the template on the x- or y-axis. A template does not support a crop box or orientation angle. When placing a template on a page that was created from a PDF page, the coordinate origin must be adjusted according to the original media or crop box. In addition, the template must maybe be drawn into a clipping path (if the original page contained a crop box), and the template must maybe also be rotated so that the original orientation can be preserved. All these considerations complicate the handling of imported templates. To make the usage of imported templates easier the function considers the coordinate origin, it draws the template automatically into a clipping rectangle if required, and it rotates the template according to the original page orientation. The only thing that must be considered is that the original size of the template must be calculated from the crop box if present. In addition, the so computed width and height must be exchanged if the original page orientation was 90, -90, 270, or -270 degrees. See also PlaceTemplateEx function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.BeginTemplate DynaPDF.CreateAnnotAP DynaPDF.DeleteTemplate DynaPDF.DeleteTemplateEx DynaPDF.ImportPageAsTemplate DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.PlaceTemplate DynaPDF.RotateTemplate DynaPDF.SetPageCoords Example Databases DynaPDF/Apply Letter Paper to PDF DynaPDF/Convert to 2 Pages DynaPDF/Generate Previews DynaPDF/Place Template DynaPDF/Shrink PDF pages Blog Entries Adding cutting lines for PDF in FileMaker Create two page PDF document in FileMaker FileMaker Magazin Ausgabe 4/2022, Seite 34 This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 22nd April 2015 DynaPDF.PlaceTemplate - DynaPDF.Print 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: TemplateHandle
parameters.description: The handle value returned from DynaPDF.ImportPageAsTemplate function.

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: The x coordinate.

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: The y coordinate. Please notice that PDF coordinates start at bottom of page except you change that with DynaPDF.SetPageCoords functions.

parameters.name: W
parameters.description: The width of the destination rectangle.

parameters.name: H
parameters.description: The height of the destination rectangle.

functions.name: DynaPDF.Print
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.Print"; PDF { ; PrinterName; TempPath; DocName; PrintFlags; MarginLeft; MarginTop; MarginRight; MarginBottom; ShowDialog; MaxRes } )
functions.description: Prints the PDF file that is currently in memory. At time of publication the function is implemented on Windows only and pages are printed as image. The parameter DocName is optional but should be set if possible. The document name is set to "out" if no name is provided. Output color format It is possible to determine which color formats a printer supports as input, but it is not possible to determine whether a printer outputs black & white, gray shades, or color. The default should be set to gray or 1 bit but the user should be able to configure the color format. Maximum output resolution The maximum output resolution is 600 DPI to restrict the memory usage and to reduce the amount of data that must be send to the printer.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: PrinterName
parameters.description: Printer name. Ignored if dialog is shown. See Printer.PrinterNames functiomn for printer names. If empty, we take it from printer settings.

parameters.name: TempPath
parameters.description: The path to temporary folder. Can be empty for default folder.

parameters.name: DocName
parameters.description: The name of the print job.

parameters.name: PrintFlags
parameters.description: The flags for printing. Can be a combination of Default, 1bit, Color, AutoRotateAndCenter, PrintAsImage or ShrinkToPrintArea. Can be just the text name of an option or the sum of the numbers.

parameters.name: MarginLeft
parameters.description: The margin for the left for the page. Pass zero if you don't need an extra margin.

parameters.name: MarginTop
parameters.description: The margin for the top for the page.

parameters.name: MarginRight
parameters.description: The margin for the right for the page.

parameters.name: MarginBottom
parameters.description: The margin for the bottom for the page.

parameters.name: ShowDialog
parameters.description: Whether to show dialog. Default is 0 as we want no dialog. Pass 1 to show a dialog here.

parameters.name: MaxRes
parameters.description: The maximum resolution to use. If value is zero or empty, we use default print resolution.

examples.label: Prints a PDF to printer
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "DynaPDF.Print";
$PDF
; "MyPrinter")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.Print"; $PDF; "MyPrinter")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

;
$destpage

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.IsInitialized")  ≠  1</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="1" name="Perform Script">
    <Script id="99999" name="InitDynaPDF"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Clear current PDF document</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.New")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pdf</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; $pdf; Print PDFs::InputPDF)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>

    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

Sets device mode. Sets the print settings for printing on Windows with DynaPDF.Print function. See also Print function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Print with options Copy Text Copy XML #Initialize DynaPDF if needed If [ MBS (" DynaPDF.IsInitialized ")  ≠  1] Perform Script [“InitDynaPDF”] End If #New PDF session Set Variable [ $pdf ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.New ")] #Load PDF from container Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer "; $pdf ; Print PDFs::InputPDF )] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile "; $pdf ; 1)] #Print Set Variable [ $flags ; Value:If ( Position ( Print PDFs::Options ; "ShrinkToPrintArea"; 1; 1)>0; 16; 0) + If ( Position ( Print PDFs::Options ; "AutoRotateAndCenter"; 1; 1)>0; 4; 0) + If ( Position ( Print PDFs::Options ; "Color"; 1; 1)>0; 2; 0) + If ( …] Set Variable [ $ShowDialog ; Value:Position ( Print PDFs::Options ; "ShowDialog"; 1; 1)>0] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( "DynaPDF.Print.SetDevMode"; Print PDFs::Printer Device Mode)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.Print.SetDevNames "; Print PDFs::Printer Device Names)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Print "; $pdf ; Print PDFs::Printer Name; ""; ""; $flags ; ""; ""; ""; ""; $ShowDialog )] Set Field [ Print PDFs::Printer Device Names; MBS ( " DynaPDF.Print.GetDevNames " )] Set Field [ Print PDFs::Printer Device Mode; MBS (" DynaPDF.Print.GetDevMode ")] #Cleanup Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Release "; $pdf )] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

parameters.name: DeviceNames
parameters.description: The list of device names. First item in list is optional driver name. Second item is optional devicen name. Third item is optional output port name. Forth item is optional flags value. Pass empty string to clear it.

functions.name: DynaPDF.Print.SetSetting
functions.description: Sets a print setting. Value can be empty (or -1) to clear value. PrinterName: A text that specifies the "friendly" name of the printer or display; for example, "PCL/HP LaserJet" in the case of PCL/HP LaserJet. This string is unique among device drivers. Note that this name may be truncated to fit in the field. Can be overwritten via parameter to DynaPDF.Print ! Orientation: Value 1 is portrait and 2 is landscape. PaperSize: Selects the size of the paper to print on. This member can be set to zero if the length and width of the paper are both set by the PaperLength and PaperWidth members. Otherwise, the PaperSize member can be set to a device specific value greater than or equal to DMPAPER_USER or to one of the following predefined values.

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: What propert to set. This includes PrinterName, Orientation, PaperSize, PaperLength, PaperWidth, Scale, Copies, DefaultSource, PrintQuality, Color, Duplex, YResolution, TTOption, Collate, FormName, MediaType, and DitherType.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value to set. Can be empty for most values to clear it.

functions.name: DynaPDF.RGB
functions.description: Calculates color value for RGB. For use with functions which take a color as a big number. Calculation is Blue * 65536 + Green * 256 + Red. Use it only if the color space is RGB.

parameters.name: Red
parameters.description: The red color component from 0 to 255.

parameters.name: Green
parameters.description: The green color component from 0 to 255.

parameters.name: Blue
parameters.description: The blue color component from 0 to 255.

examples.label: Get a blue color value:
examples.text_code:

( "DynaPDF.RGB"; 0; 0; 255 )

functions.name: DynaPDF.ReadImageFormat
functions.description:
The function retrieves the most important properties of an image file. The function reads only the image header to improve processing speed. If the parameter BitsPerPixel is 32 the image is a CMYK image. Note that TIFF images support color depths up to 64 bits per pixel. The parameter Index specifies the array index of a multi-page image that should be read in; numbering starts at 1. The parameter is ignored for non-multi-page image formats. Image format is returned as a text with width, height and bits per pixel separated by newline. See also ReadImageFormat function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Place an image centered on the page with border: Copy Text Copy XML Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.AppendPage "; $pdf ) ] # put a background in Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetFillColor "; $pdf ; ,8; ,8; ,8) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Rectangle "; $pdf ; 0; 0; MBS ( " DynaPDF.GetPageWidth "; $PDF ); MBS ( " DynaPDF.GetPageHeight "; $PDF ); "fill") ] # options for saving image Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetSaveNewImageFormat "; $pdf ; 0) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetResolution "; $pdf ; 300) ] # read size of image Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ("DynaPDF.ReadImageFormat"; $pdf ; Merge PDFs::InputImage ) ] Set Variable [ $ImageWidth ; Value: GetAsNumber (LeftValues ( $r ; 1 )) ] Set Variable [ $imageHeight ; Value: GetAsNumber (MiddleValues ( $r ; 2; 1 )) ] # The destination area where to put PDF Set Variable [ $TargetX ; Value: 50 ] Set Variable [ $TargetY ; Value: 50 ] Set Variable [ $TargetWidth ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.GetPageWidth "; $PDF ) - 100 ] Set Variable [ $TargetHeight ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.GetPageHeight "; $PDF ) - 100 ] # calculate scale factor Set Variable [ $factor ; Value: Min( $TargetHeight / $ImageHeight ; $TargetWidth / $ImageWidth ) ] # calculate output size Set Variable [ $DestWidth ; Value: $ImageWidth * $factor ] Set Variable [ $DestHeight ; Value: $ImageHeight * $factor ] # Center in target area Set Variable [ $DestX ; Value: $TargetX + ( $TargetWidth - $DestWidth ) / 2 ] Set Variable [ $DestY ; Value: $TargetY + ( $TargetHeight - $DestHeight ) / 2 ] # insert the image Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.InsertImage "; $pdf ; Merge PDFs::InputImage ; $DestX ; $DestY ; $DestWidth ; $DestHeight ) ] # close page and move to next record Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.EndPage "; $pdf ) ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.AppendPage"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> put a background in</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SetFillColor"; $pdf; ,8; ,8; ,8) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.Rectangle"; $pdf; 0; 0; MBS( "DynaPDF.GetPageWidth"; $PDF ); MBS( "DynaPDF.GetPageHeight"; $PDF ); "fill") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> options for saving image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SetSaveNewImageFormat"; $pdf; 0) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SetResolution"; $pdf; 300) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> read size of image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ReadImageFormat"; $pdf; Merge PDFs::InputImage) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetAsNumber (LeftValues ( $r ; 1 )) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ImageWidth</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetAsNumber (MiddleValues ( $r ; 2; 1 )) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$imageHeight</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> The destination area where to put PDF</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>50 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$TargetX</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>50 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$TargetY</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.GetPageWidth"; $PDF ) - 100 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$TargetWidth</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.GetPageHeight"; $PDF ) - 100 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$TargetHeight</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> calculate scale factor</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Min( $TargetHeight / $ImageHeight; $TargetWidth / $ImageWidth) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$factor</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> calculate output size</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$ImageWidth * $factor </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$DestWidth</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$ImageHeight * $factor </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$DestHeight</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Center in target area</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$TargetX + ($TargetWidth-$DestWidth) / 2 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$DestX</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$TargetY + ($TargetHeight-$DestHeight) / 2 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$DestY</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> insert the image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.InsertImage"; $pdf; Merge PDFs::InputImage; $DestX; $DestY; $DestWidth; $DestHeight) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> close page and move to next record</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.EndPage"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> See also DynaPDF.AppendImagePage DynaPDF.AppendPage DynaPDF.GetPageHeight DynaPDF.GetPageWidth DynaPDF.InsertImage DynaPDF.ReadImageFormatFile DynaPDF.SetFillColor DynaPDF.SetPageFormat DynaPDF.SetResolution DynaPDF.SetSaveNewImageFormat Release notes Version 15.2 Added DynaPDF.ReadImageFormatFile and DynaPDF.ReadImageResolutionFile function to inspect images files on disk. Version 14.5 Improved DynaPDF.GeneratePreview , DynaPDF.ReadImageResolution , DynaPDF.ReadImageFormat , DynaPDF.InsertImage , DynaPDF.AttachFileContainer , DynaPDF.AddOutputIntentEx , DynaPDF.CreateICCBasedColorSpaceContainer and DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer to return proper error if container is empty. functions.name: DynaPDF.ReadImageFormatFile
functions.description: The function retrieves the most important properties of an image file. The function reads only the image header to improve processing speed. If the parameter BitsPerPixel is 32 the image is a CMYK image. Note that TIFF images support color depths up to 64 bits per pixel. The parameter Index specifies the array index of a multi-page image that should be read in; numbering starts at 1. The parameter is ignored for non-multi-page image formats. Image format is returned as a text with width, height and bits per pixel separated by newline. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native path for the image file.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the image for multi image files.

functions.result: Returns image format or error message.
