functions.name: DynaPDF.ReadImageResolution
functions.description: The function retrieves the horizontal and vertical resolution of an image file. The image resolution is a user defined value that can be stored in certain image formats such as Bitmap or TIFF. The function returns zero values if the image format does not support a resolution record or if no values are stored in the image file, the image resolution is then 72 DPI. See also ReadImageResolution function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.AppendImagePage DynaPDF.AppendImagePages DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.ReadImageResolutionFile Release notes Version 15.2 Added DynaPDF.ReadImageFormatFile and DynaPDF.ReadImageResolutionFile function to inspect images files on disk. Version 14.5 Improved DynaPDF.GeneratePreview , DynaPDF.ReadImageResolution , DynaPDF.ReadImageFormat , DynaPDF.InsertImage , DynaPDF.AttachFileContainer , DynaPDF.AddOutputIntentEx , DynaPDF.CreateICCBasedColorSpaceContainer and DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer to return proper error if container is empty. Example Databases DynaPDF/Picture to PDF OCR/OCR with DynaPDF Blog Entries MBS FileMaker Plugin, version 14.5pr8 This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 DynaPDF.ReadImageFormatFile - DynaPDF.ReadImageResolutionFile 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The image container value. Pass the container field or a variable with the container content.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the image for multi image files.

functions.result: Returns the x and y resolution values as text separated by newline character.

functions.name: DynaPDF.ReadImageResolutionFile
functions.description: The function retrieves the horizontal and vertical resolution of an image file. The image resolution is a user defined value that can be stored in certain image formats such as Bitmap or TIFF. The function returns zero values if the image format does not support a resolution record or if no values are stored in the image file, the image resolution is then 72 DPI. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native path for the image file.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the image for multi image files.

functions.result: Returns the x and y resolution values as text separated by newline character.

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Draws a rectangle: Copy Text Copy XML MBS ( "DynaPDF.Rectangle"; $PDF ; 100; 200; 200; 200; "stroke" ) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Find text and draw red rectangle around: Copy Text Copy XML Set Variable [ $pos ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.FindText "; $pdf ; Links::Text ; 1) ] If [ IsEmpty ( $pos ) ] # not found Else #  read positions Set Variable [ $pos ; Value: Substitute( $pos ; " "; ¶) ] Set Variable [ $x1 ; Value: MBS ( " Math.TextToNumber "; GetValue( $pos ; 1)) ] Set Variable [ $y1 ; Value: MBS ( " Math.TextToNumber "; GetValue( $pos ; 2)) ] Set Variable [ $x2 ; Value: MBS ( " Math.TextToNumber "; GetValue( $pos ; 3)) ] Set Variable [ $xy ; Value: MBS ( " Math.TextToNumber "; GetValue( $pos ; 4)) ] Set Variable [ $x3 ; Value: MBS ( " Math.TextToNumber "; GetValue( $pos ; 5)) ] Set Variable [ $y3 ; Value: MBS ( " Math.TextToNumber "; GetValue( $pos ; 6)) ] Set Variable [ $x4 ; Value: MBS ( " Math.TextToNumber "; GetValue( $pos ; 7)) ] Set Variable [ $y4 ; Value: MBS ( " Math.TextToNumber "; GetValue( $pos ; 8)) ] Set Variable [ $w ; Value: MBS ( " Math.TextToNumber "; $x3 - $x1 ) ] Set Variable [ $h ; Value: MBS ( " Math.TextToNumber "; $y3 - $y1 ) ] # # draw red rectangle Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.SaveGraphicState "; $pdf ) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.SetStrokeColor "; $pdf ; 1; 0; 0) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( "DynaPDF.Rectangle"; $pdf ; $x1 ; $y1 ; $w ; $h ; "stroke" ) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.RestoreGraphicState "; $pdf ) ] End If <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

functions.name: DynaPDF.Release
functions.description: Releases a PDF reference. This will free all memory used for this PDF instance.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

functions.name: DynaPDF.ReleaseAll
functions.description: Frees all DynaPDF objects. Be aware that on a server this may free objects, which are used by other scripts running in parallel!

functions.name: DynaPDF.RenameSpotColor
functions.description: Renames a spot color. Renames the spot color Colorant to NewName in all Separation, DeviceN, and NChannel color spaces in which the colorant can be found. If the parameter NewName is set to empty, the function renames the colorant to the special value None which produces no visible output. The representation of the colorant in the alternate color space is left unchanged, also if the colorant is set to the special value None or All. The function returns the number of color spaces in which the colorant has been changed. If the function fails the return value is the error message. See also RenameSpotColor function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.New Release notes Version 7.2 Added DynaPDF.ChangeSeparationColor and DynaPDF.RenameSpotColor . Example Databases DynaPDF/Change Separation Colorant Blog Entries MBS FileMaker Plugin, version 7.2pr1 This function checks for a license. Created 22nd March 2017 , last changed 22nd March 2017 DynaPDF.ReleaseAll - DynaPDF.RenderJob 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: Colorant
parameters.description: Colorant name to change.

parameters.name: NewName
parameters.description: New name or empty.

functions.name: DynaPDF.RenderJob
functions.description: Renders PDF pages in the background. This allows you to specify a JSON with the job and run it in background in FileMaker Pro or synchronously in FileMaker Server. For synchronous mode, we return a JSON and otherwise pass it to the script triggered as parameter. Input parameters are show in the first table and result/script parameter JSON is shown in the second table.

parameters.name: Job
parameters.description: The job data as JSON.

parameters.name: InputContainer
parameters.description: Input PDF as a container. Alternatively pass InputPath in the job JSON.

parameters.name: Sync
parameters.description: Pass 1 to run the job synchronously. Server side always runs synchronously. But client runs by default asynchronously.

functions.name: DynaPDF.RenderPDFFile
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.RenderPDFFile"; PDF { ; DestPath; Resolution; Flags; PixelFormat; Filter; Format } )
functions.description:
Renders whole PDF file in current PDF to an image. You can choose the format and filter, but please make sure they match as not all combinations are valid. This can write multipage tiff files or a folder of image files. Requires DynaPDF Pro license for the raster engine to create bitmaps from PDF pages. Please use DynaPDF.InitColorManagement or DynaPDF.InitColorManagementEx to initialize color management, so conversions to other colorspaces like CMYK work better. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer. See also RenderPDFFile function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Render PDF to desktop with several JPEG pages: Copy Text Copy XML $result = MBS ( "DynaPDF.RenderPDFFile"; $PDF ; "/Users/cs/Desktop"; 150; 0; "RGB"; "JPEG"; "JPEG" ) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.RenderPDFFile"; $PDF; "/Users/cs/Desktop"; 150; 0; "RGB"; "JPEG"; "JPEG" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Render TIFF with higher resolution: Copy Text Copy XML $result = MBS ( "DynaPDF.RenderPDFFile"; $PDF ; "c:\test.tif"; 150; 0; "RGB"; "Flate"; "TIFF" ) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.RenderPDFFile"; $PDF; "c:\test.tif"; 150; 0; "RGB"; "Flate"; "TIFF" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Open PDF and render page as image to container: Copy Text Copy XML # Start new PDF workspace Set Variable [ $pdf ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.New ")] # Load PDF from container Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer "; $pdf ; Test::data )] # Import all pages Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile "; $pdf )] # Render one page as Picture Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( "DynaPDF.RenderPDFFile"; $PDF ; "c:\test.tif"; 150; 0; "RGB"; "Flate"; "TIFF" ) )] # Put in Container Set Field [ Test::PageImage ; $r ] # cleanup Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Release "; $pdf )] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Start new PDF workspace</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.New")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pdf</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Load PDF from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; $pdf; Test::data)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Import all pages</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile"; $pdf)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Render one page as Picture</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.RenderPDFFile"; $PDF; "c:\test.tif"; 150; 0; "RGB"; "Flate"; "TIFF" ) )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Put in Container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$r</Calculation>
    <Field table="Test" id="99999" name="PageImage"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.Release"; $pdf)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> See also DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile DynaPDF.InitColorManagement DynaPDF.InitColorManagementEx DynaPDF.Initialize DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer DynaPDF.Release DynaPDF.RenderPage DynaPDF.RenderPDFFileEx DynaPDF.SetOCGState Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath Release notes Version 15.2 Added PremulAlpha as a possible raster flag for DynaPDF.RenderPage , DynaPDF.RenderPDFFile and DynaPDF.RenderPDFFileEx functions. Version 8.0 Added DynaPDF.RenderPDFFileEx , DynaPDF.FileAttachAnnotEx and DynaPDF.ExtractText . parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: DestPath
parameters.description: A file path for storing images. Can be folder for multiple images files or file for one file. If a folder, it must exist.

parameters.name: Resolution
parameters.description: The resolution you'd like to have for the image. If you pass zero, we use default resolution (72).

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Flags for rendering. Use 0 for the default flags. For other values, please look into dynapdf manual. With version 6.4 of our plugin, you can also specify this by passing in text string, e.g. "Rotate90¶ClipToTrimBox"

parameters.name: PixelFormat
parameters.description: The pixel format. Can be 1bit, gray, RGB, BGR, RGBA, BGRA, ARGB, ABGR, CMYK, CMYKA and GrayA. Default is RGB.

parameters.name: Filter
parameters.description: The compression filter to use. Can be Flate, JPEG, CCITT3, CCITT4, LZW or JP2K. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: Format
parameters.description: The image format to use. Can be TIFF, JPEG, PNG, BMP or JPC. Default is JPEG.

functions.name: DynaPDF.RenderPDFFileEx
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.RenderPDFFileEx"; PDF { ; DestPath; Resolution; Width; Height; Flags; PixelFormat; Filter; Format } )
functions.description: Renders whole PDF file in current PDF to an image. The pages in memory could be imported from one or more external PDF files, e.g. with ImportPDFFile(), created with DynaPDF functions, or a combination of both. The parameter DestPath can be a path to an existing directory or the file name of the output image. The latter type can be used with TIFF images because this format supports multi-page output. When a file path is used with a single page image format only the first page will be rendered. The function checks whether the path is a directory or a file name. The function can render pages in a specific resolution, or scale them to a given width or height. Depending on which parameters are set the image size is calculated as follows: Resolution > 0 and Width == 0 and Height == 0: Pages are rendered according to the given resolution. Note that PDF pages can be very large. Therefore, it is maybe not possible to render all pages in the wished resolution. Resolution > 0 and Width < 0 and or Height < 0: Pages are rendered according to the given resolution. Negative values of Width and Height are interpreted as maximum width or height if Resolution is greater zero. Since PDF pages can be very large, it is recommended to set the maximum width and height to a value that is low enough so that no out of memory exception occurs, e.g. 5000 x 5000 pixels. Resolution == 0 and Width > 0 or Height > 0: Pages are scaled to the given Width or Height. If Width and Height are greater zero then pages are scaled to that size independent of the original page format (not recommended). It is usually best to set the width or height to zero so that the function can calculate the missing value to preserve the aspect ratio. On a 32 bit system it is possible to render PDF pages in RGB with up to around 1200 DPI, depending on the page format and available memory. The resolution of gray images can be higher but the encoder must be able to handle such large images. The PNG and bitmap encoders accept images in almost arbitrary resolutions but all other encoders can fail when the resolution is larger than about 2000 DPI. Requires DynaPDF Pro license for the raster engine to create bitmaps from PDF pages. Please use DynaPDF.InitColorManagement or DynaPDF.InitColorManagementEx to initialize color management, so conversions to other colorspaces like CMYK work better. See also RenderPDFFileEx function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.InitColorManagement DynaPDF.InitColorManagementEx DynaPDF.Initialize DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.RenderPDFFile Release notes Version 15.2 Added PremulAlpha as a possible raster flag for DynaPDF.RenderPage , DynaPDF.RenderPDFFile and DynaPDF.RenderPDFFileEx functions. Version 8.0 Added DynaPDF.RenderPDFFileEx , DynaPDF.FileAttachAnnotEx and DynaPDF.ExtractText . Created 21st December 2017 , last changed 17th July 2022 DynaPDF.RenderPDFFile - DynaPDF.RenderPage 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: DynaPDF.RenderPage
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.RenderPage"; PDF; PageIndex { ; Resolution; Width; Height; Flags; PixelFormat; Filter; Format; FileName; DestPath } )
functions.description:
Renders a page in current PDF to an image. You can choose the format and filter, but please make sure they match as not all combinations are valid. Requires DynaPDF Pro license for the raster engine to create bitmaps from PDF pages. Please use DynaPDF.InitColorManagement or DynaPDF.InitColorManagementEx to initialize color management, so conversions to other colorspaces like CMYK work better. You can use DynaPDF.SetPageBBox or DynaPDF.SetBBox to clip the page with the crop box and render only that portion. If the whole page is just a big picture, you could use DynaPDF.GetImage function to extract it. That would prevent scaling or recompression. See also RenderPage function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Render page 5 with default options: Copy Text Copy XML $ImageData = MBS ( "DynaPDF.RenderPage"; $PDF ; 5 ) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.RenderPage"; $PDF; 5 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ImageData</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Render page 5 with higher resolution: Copy Text Copy XML $ImageData = MBS ( "DynaPDF.RenderPage"; $PDF ; 5; 150 ) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.RenderPage"; $PDF; 5; 150 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ImageData</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Render page 5 to file: Copy Text Copy XML $result = MBS ( "DynaPDF.RenderPage"; $PDF ; 5; 150; 0; 0; 0; "RGB"; "JPEG"; "JPEG"; ""; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.jpg" ) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.RenderPage"; $PDF; 5; 150; 0; 0; 0; "RGB"; "JPEG"; "JPEG"; ""; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.jpg" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Open PDF and render page as image to container: Copy Text Copy XML # Start new PDF workspace Set Variable [ $pdf ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.New ")] # Load PDF from container Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer "; $pdf ; Test::data )] # Import all pages Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile "; $pdf )] # Render one page as Picture Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ("DynaPDF.RenderPage"; $pdf ; Test::PageIndex )] # Put in Container Set Field [ Test::PageImage ; $r ] # cleanup Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Release "; $pdf )] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

</fmxmlsnippet> Render in one formula: Copy Text Copy XML Let ( // input: PDFDocumentContainer [ ~PDFObject = MBS (" DynaPDF.New "); ~error = MBS ("IsError"); ~x = MBS (" DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer "; ~PDFObject; PDFDocumentContainer); ~error = ~error OR MBS ("IsError"); ~x = MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage "; ~PDFObject; 1); ~error = ~error OR MBS ("IsError"); ~PageIndex = 1; ~Resolution = 150 ; ~Width = 0; ~Height = 0; ~Flags = 0; ~PixelFormat = "RGB"; ~Filter = "JPEG"; ~Format = "PNG"; ~Result = MBS ("DynaPDF.RenderPage"; ~PDFObject; ~PageIndex ; ~Resolution; ~Width; ~Height; ~Flags; ~PixelFormat; ~Filter; ~Format); ~error = ~error OR MBS ("IsError"); ~x = MBS (" DynaPDF.Release "; ~PDFObject)]; If(~error; ""; ~Result)) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( 
// input: PDFDocumentContainer
[ 
~PDFObject = MBS("DynaPDF.New");
~error = MBS("IsError");

~x = MBS("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; ~PDFObject; PDFDocumentContainer); 
~error = ~error OR MBS("IsError");

~x = MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage"; ~PDFObject; 1); 
~error = ~error OR MBS("IsError");

~PageIndex = 1;
~Resolution = 150 ;
~Width = 0;
~Height = 0;
~Flags = 0;
~PixelFormat = "RGB";
~Filter = "JPEG";
~Format = "PNG";
~Result = MBS("DynaPDF.RenderPage"; ~PDFObject; ~PageIndex ; ~Resolution; ~Width; ~Height; ~Flags; ~PixelFormat; ~Filter; ~Format);
~error = ~error OR MBS("IsError");

~x = MBS("DynaPDF.Release"; ~PDFObject)]; 
If(~error; ""; ~Result))</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Render page as CMYK to tiff file: Copy Text Copy XML Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ("DynaPDF.RenderPage"; $pdf ; 1; 300; 0; 0; "default"; "CMYK"; "Flate"; "Tiff"; ""; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.tif") ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

</fmxmlsnippet> Loop over pages and make images: Copy Text Copy XML # Start new PDF workspace Set Variable [ $pdf ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.New ")] # Load PDF from container Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer "; $pdf ; Test::data )] # Import all pages Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile "; $pdf )] # loop counting up from 1 to $count Set Variable [ $count ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.GetPageCount "; $pdf ) ] Set Variable [ $index ; Value: 1 ] If [ $index ≤ $count ] Loop # Render one page as Picture Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ("DynaPDF.RenderPage"; $pdf ; $index )] # Put in Container New Record/Request Set Field [ Test::PageImage ; $r ] Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: Off ] # next Set Variable [ $index ; Value: $index + 1 ] Exit Loop If [ $index > $count ] End Loop End If # cleanup Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Release "; $pdf )] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Load PDF from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; $pdf; Test::data)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Import all pages</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile"; $pdf)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> loop counting up from 1 to $count</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.GetPageCount"; $pdf ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $index ≤ $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Render one page as Picture</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.RenderPage"; $pdf; $index)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Put in Container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$r</Calculation>
    <Field table="Test" id="99999" name="PageImage"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $index > $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.Release"; $pdf)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: PageIndex
parameters.description: The page number between 1 and DynaPDF.GetPageCount .

parameters.name: Resolution
parameters.description: The resolution you'd like to have for the image. If you pass zero, we use default resolution (72).

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: The width of the output image. Can be zero to use page width or to scale based on height.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: The height of the output image. Can be zero to use page height or to scale based on width.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Flags for rendering. Use 0 for the default flags. For other values, please look into dynapdf manual. With version 6.4 of our plugin, you can also specify this by passing in text string, e.g. "Rotate90¶ClipToTrimBox" Valid flags: Default, ScaleToMediaBox, IgnoreCropBox, ClipToArtBox, ClipToBleedBox, ClipToTrimBox, ExclAnnotations, ExclFormFields, SkipUpdateBG, Rotate90, Rotate180, Rotate270, InitBlack, CompositeWhite, ExclPageContent, ExclButtons, ExclCheckBoxes, ExclComboBoxes, ExclListBoxes, ExclTextFields, ExclSigFields, ScaleToBBox, DisableAAClipping, DisableAAText, DisableAAVector, DisableAntiAliasing, DisableBiLinearFilter, ClipBoxMask, RenderInvisibleText. Added EnableBlendCS as flag in v12.3.

parameters.name: PixelFormat
parameters.description: The pixel format. Can be 1bit, gray, RGB, BGR, RGBA, BGRA, ARGB, ABGR, CMYK, CMYKA and GrayA. Default is RGB.

parameters.name: Filter
parameters.description: The compression filter to use. Can be Flate, JPEG, CCITT3, CCITT4, LZW or JP2K. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: Format
parameters.description: The image format to use. Can be TIFF, JPEG, PNG, BMP or JPC. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name to use for the container value. Default is page with right file extensions for image. Pass any value if you use DestPath parameter.

parameters.name: DestPath
parameters.description: Optional, a file path for storing image instead of a container.

functions.result: Returns container value with image or error message.

examples.label: Render in one formula:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( 
// input: PDFDocumentContainer
[ 
~PDFObject = MBS("DynaPDF.New");
~error = MBS("IsError");

~x = MBS("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; ~PDFObject; PDFDocumentContainer); 
~error = ~error OR MBS("IsError");

~x = MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFPage"; ~PDFObject; 1); 
~error = ~error OR MBS("IsError");

~PageIndex = 1;
~Resolution = 150 ;
~Width = 0;
~Height = 0;
~Flags = 0;
~PixelFormat = "RGB";
~Filter = "JPEG";
~Format = "PNG";
~Result = MBS("DynaPDF.RenderPage"; ~PDFObject; ~PageIndex ; ~Resolution; ~Width; ~Height; ~Flags; ~PixelFormat; ~Filter; ~Format);
~error = ~error OR MBS("IsError");

~x = MBS("DynaPDF.Release"; ~PDFObject)]; 
If(~error; ""; ~Result))</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DynaPDF.ReplaceImage
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.ReplaceImage"; PDF; ImageIndex; Image { ; ImageIndex; ColorSpace; ColorSpaceHandle; Flags } )
functions.description:
Replaces an image with another image. The resolution, aspect ratio, color space and so on can be freely chosen. However, note that this function does not change the output position or size. If the aspect ratio of the new image is different, then it will be stretched or shrunken to fit into the output rectangle. The parameter CS specifies the destination color space into which the image should be converted or saved, if the image is already defined in that color space. CSHandle must be the handle of that color space if a non-device space is used. The will be ignored for devices spaces. The function works in the very same way as DynaPDF.InsertImage with the following differences: The color of an image mask cannot be set or changed because this would require changes on the content stream in which the image is used. An image mask will be created if the image color depth is 1 bit and if color key masking is enabled (see SetUseTransparency()). SetUseTransparency() should normally be set to false before calling this function. The image will never be downscaled, independent of the current resolution, because the size of the output rectangle is not known.  If the image that should be replaced is a soft mask of another base image, then make sure that the destination color space is set to DeviceGray, CalGray, or to a one channel ICC based color space because a soft mask must not contain more than one color channel.  The flags UseImageColorSpace, IgnoreICCProfiles, RealPassThrough, and NoBitmapAlpha are all supported. See DynaPDF.SetGStateFlags for further information. Needs DynaPDF Lite license. See also ReplaceImage function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Replace image: Copy Text Copy XML # Initialize DynaPDF if needed If [ MBS (" DynaPDF.IsInitialized ")  ≠  1 ] Perform Script [ “InitDynaPDF” ] End If # Start new PDF session Set Variable [ $pdf ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.New ") ] # Import pages Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer "; $pdf ; Replace Image::Input PDF) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile "; $pdf ; 1) ] # Replace Image Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ("DynaPDF.ReplaceImage"; $pdf ; 0; Replace Image::New Image) ] # Save PDF Set Field [ Replace Image::Output PDF ; MBS (" DynaPDF.Save "; $pdf ) ] # Cleanup Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.Release "; $pdf ) ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

  </Step>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.New") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pdf</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; $pdf; Replace Image::Input PDF) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile"; $pdf; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Replace Image</Text>
  </Step>

    </Value>

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: ImageIndex
parameters.description: The index of the image to replace. From 0 to DynaPDF.GetImageCount -1.

parameters.name: Image
parameters.description: The new image. Can be container or native file path.

parameters.name: ImageIndex
parameters.description: The index of the image. This is for a multi picture tiff file to pick the image. Default is 1 for first image.

parameters.name: ColorSpace
parameters.description: The color space to use. Can be DeviceRGB, DeviceCMYK, DeviceGray, CalGray, CalRGB, Lab, ICCBased, Pattern, Indexed, Separation, DeviceN or NChannel. Default is DeviceRGB.

parameters.name: ColorSpaceHandle
parameters.description: The color space handle. Can be empty if you want to use device colorspace.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: The flags. Can be number or text with various flags. Can be set to DeleteAltImages, DeleteMetadata, DeleteOCG and/or DeleteSoftMask.

functions.name: DynaPDF.ReplacePattern
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.ReplacePattern"; PDF; Text; NewText { ; Alignment; NewFontSize; NewFontStyle; NewCharacterSpacing; NewTextScaling; NewTextRise; NewTextColor } )
functions.description: Replaces a text with a new text. You need to have an open page. you can use DynaPDF.EditPage to edit an existing one. Needs DynaPDF Pro license. The replace pattern function is great to replace small place holder with real values in a text. But it doesn't do much formatting except parameters below and won't work with multi line texts. For bigger texts, please consider drawing a white rectangle and then use DynaPDF.WriteStyledText or similar functions to draw text on top. See also newer functions DynaPDF.Parser.FindText and DynaPDF.Parser.ReplaceSelText .

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to search.

parameters.name: NewText
parameters.description: The text to replace the found location with.

parameters.name: Alignment
parameters.description: The new alignment you want. 0 = Left, 1 = Center and 2 = Right. May not work with rotated text.

parameters.name: NewFontSize
parameters.description: The new font size to use. Use -1 to keep whatever it is. Due to matrix scaling in pdf, the size here may be far off.

parameters.name: NewFontStyle
parameters.description: The new font style. Use -1 to keep whatever it is. e.g. 4 for underline, 2 for bold, 1 for italic or a combination. See dynapdf manual for more values for font styles.

parameters.name: NewCharacterSpacing
parameters.description: The new character spacing value. See also DynaPDF.SetCharacterSpacing . If undefined, keeps current character spacing.

parameters.name: NewTextScaling
parameters.description: The text scaling to use.

parameters.name: NewTextRise
parameters.description: The text rise to use. In percent of text height as plugin multipies y by given factor.

parameters.name: NewTextColor
parameters.description: The new stroke & fill color. Pass negative number to keep existing color.

functions.name: DynaPDF.ResetAnnotAP
functions.description: Forces a rebuild of the appearance stream of the specified annotation. If Handle is set to -1 all annotation appearances will be rebuild. This function be useful if an imported annotation has an invalid or empty appearance stream. See also ResetAnnotAP function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.CreateAnnotAP DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.TextAnnot Release notes Version 10.3 Added DynaPDF.FlushPagesEx and DynaPDF.ResetAnnotAP functions. Blog Entries MBS FileMaker Plugin, version 10.3pr9 This function checks for a license. Created 14th July 2020 , last changed 14th July 2020 DynaPDF.ReplacePattern - DynaPDF.ResetEncryptionSettings 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: Handle
parameters.description: The Annotation handle. If not passed, we use -1 to rebuild all.

functions.name: DynaPDF.ResetEncryptionSettings
functions.description: Resets the encryption settings which were imported from an external PDF file so that the PDF file in memory can be saved unencrypted. If the function succeeds the return value is 1. If the function fails the return value is 0. See also ResetEncryptionSettings function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.ImportEncryptionSettings DynaPDF.New This function checks for a license. Created 19th January 2017 , last changed 19th January 2017 DynaPDF.ResetAnnotAP - DynaPDF.RestoreGraphicState 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.description: The function restores a previously saved graphics state. See also DynaPDF.SaveGraphicState . See also RestoreGraphicState function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.ClipPath DynaPDF.CreateRadialShading DynaPDF.DrawCircle DynaPDF.RotateCoords DynaPDF.Save DynaPDF.SaveGraphicState DynaPDF.ScaleCoords DynaPDF.SetAlpha DynaPDF.SkewCoords DynaPDF.TranslateCoords Example Databases Barcode/DynaPDF Barcode Barcode/QRCode PDF DynaPDF/Coordinates DynaPDF/Create Text as PDFA DynaPDF/Page Layouting DynaPDF/Place Template DynaPDF/Signature Appearance DynaPDF/Watermark pages Blog Entries Page Layouting in DynaPDF DynaPDF graphics flags This function checks for a license. parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

functions.name: DynaPDF.RotateCoords
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.RotateCoords"; PDF; a; X; Y { ; RotateCoords } )
functions.description:
The function skews the coordinate system and sets the coordinate origin to the point OriginX, OriginY. It is highly recommended to save the graphics state beforehand, otherwise it is very difficult or impossible to restore the coordinate system later. After the coordinate system was changed by the function, bottom-up coordinates are active. It is not possible to use top-down coordinates with a skewed coordinate system. To rotate a page you can use DynaPDF.SetOrientation or DynaPDF.SetOrientationEx . See also RotateCoords function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Rotate and draw rectangle: Copy Text Copy XML MBS ( " DynaPDF.SaveGraphicState "; $PDF ) MBS ( "DynaPDF.RotateCoords"; $PDF ; 30; 150; 450) MBS ( " DynaPDF.Rectangle "; $PDF ; 0; 0; 200; 100; "stroke" ) MBS ( " DynaPDF.RestoreGraphicState "; $PDF ) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.SaveGraphicState"; $PDF )
MBS( "DynaPDF.RotateCoords"; $PDF; 30; 150; 450)
MBS( "DynaPDF.Rectangle"; $PDF; 0; 0; 200; 100; "stroke" )
MBS( "DynaPDF.RestoreGraphicState"; $PDF )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Rotate graphics system 270° for whole page: Copy Text Copy XML MBS (" DynaPDF.TranslateCoords "; $pdf ; MBS (" DynaPDF.GetPageWidth "; $pdf )/2; MBS ( " DynaPDF.GetPageHeight "; $pdf )/2) MBS ("DynaPDF.RotateCoords"; $pdf ; 270; 0; 0) MBS (" DynaPDF.TranslateCoords "; $pdf ; - MBS ( " DynaPDF.GetPageHeight "; $pdf )/2; - MBS (" DynaPDF.GetPageWidth "; $pdf )/2) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.TranslateCoords"; $pdf; MBS("DynaPDF.GetPageWidth";$pdf)/2; MBS( "DynaPDF.GetPageHeight";$pdf)/2)
MBS("DynaPDF.RotateCoords"; $pdf; 270; 0; 0)
MBS("DynaPDF.TranslateCoords"; $pdf; -MBS( "DynaPDF.GetPageHeight";$pdf)/2; -MBS("DynaPDF.GetPageWidth";$pdf)/2)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: Angle alpha in degrees

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: Origin of the x-axis

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: Origin of the y-axis

parameters.name: RotateCoords
parameters.description: Whether to rotate coordinate system. This is now done by DynaPDF automatically, so for current version of the library, this must be zero. But older versions need the 1.

functions.result: Returns OK on success and error on failure.

examples.label: Rotate and draw rectangle:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "
DynaPDF.SaveGraphicState
";
$PDF
)
MBS
( "DynaPDF.RotateCoords";
$PDF
; 30; 150; 450)
MBS
( "
DynaPDF.Rectangle
";
$PDF
; 0; 0; 200; 100; "stroke" )
MBS
( "
DynaPDF.RestoreGraphicState
";
$PDF
)
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.SaveGraphicState"; $PDF )
MBS( "DynaPDF.RotateCoords"; $PDF; 30; 150; 450)
MBS( "DynaPDF.Rectangle"; $PDF; 0; 0; 200; 100; "stroke" )
MBS( "DynaPDF.RestoreGraphicState"; $PDF )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Rotate graphics system 270° for whole page:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.TranslateCoords"; $pdf; MBS("DynaPDF.GetPageWidth";$pdf)/2; MBS( "DynaPDF.GetPageHeight";$pdf)/2)
MBS("DynaPDF.RotateCoords"; $pdf; 270; 0; 0)
MBS("DynaPDF.TranslateCoords"; $pdf; -MBS( "DynaPDF.GetPageHeight";$pdf)/2; -MBS("DynaPDF.GetPageWidth";$pdf)/2)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DynaPDF.RotateTemplate
functions.description: Rotates a template. Returns a new template handle. This is a convenience function which creates a new template and draws the existing template there with rotation.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: Template
parameters.description: The handle to the template of a page.

parameters.name: Rotation
parameters.description: The rotation angle. Can be 0, 90, 180 or 270.

; Value:

$pdf

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Create new PDF document</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.New")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pdf</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Load PDF from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; $pdf; DynaPDF PlaceTemplate::InputPDF)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Import first page as template</Text>
  </Step>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$template</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Create 4 rotations:</Text>
  </Step>

  </Step>

# import PDF
Set Variable [

" ;
$pdf
;
Render Page::Input
PDF ) ]
Set Variable [

; Value:

( "
DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile
" ;
$pdf
) ]
# now rotate page 1

;
$angle
+ 90) ]
Set Variable [

$pdf
) ]

Image ;

" ;
$pdf
; "out.pdf" ) ]
Set Variable [

( "
DynaPDF.Release
" ;
$pdf
) ]

functions.name: DynaPDF.RoundRect
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.RoundRect"; PDF; PosX; PosY; Width; Height; Radius; FillMode )
functions.description:
The function draws a rectangle with rounded corners. The radius must not be greater than the half with or height of the rectangle. If the coordinate system is bottom-up the point PosX, PosY defines the lower left corner of the rectangle. If the coordinate system is top-down it defines the upper left corner. The draw direction can be changed with the function DynaPDF.SetDrawDirection . A rectangle is a closed path that can be filled, stroked or both. It is also possible to draw a rectangle invisible to apply the filling rules nonzero winding number or even-odd. The filling rules are described under DynaPDF.ClipPath . The parameter FillMode is ignored if the rectangle is drawn inside a clipping path. The fill modes are described under DynaPDF.ClosePath . See also RoundRect function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Draw round rectangle with blue: Copy Text Copy XML Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetStrokeColor "; $pdf ; 0; 0; 0) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetFillColor "; $pdf ; 0; 0; 0.8 ) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetLineWidth "; $pdf ; 5) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( "DynaPDF.RoundRect"; $pdf ; 100; 100; 300; 200; 20; "fillStroke" ) ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SetStrokeColor"; $pdf; 0; 0; 0) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SetFillColor"; $pdf; 0; 0; 0.8) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SetLineWidth"; $pdf; 5) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.RoundRect"; $pdf; 100; 100; 300; 200; 20; "fillStroke" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> See also DynaPDF.ClipPath DynaPDF.ClosePath DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.RoundRectEx DynaPDF.SetDrawDirection DynaPDF.SetFillColor DynaPDF.SetLineWidth DynaPDF.SetStrokeColor DynaPDF.WriteFTextEx Blog Entries MBS Filemaker Plugin, version 3.2pr4 This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 3th January 2021 DynaPDF.RotateTemplate - DynaPDF.RoundRectEx 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: PosX
parameters.description: X-Coordinate of rectangle

parameters.name: PosY
parameters.description: Y-Coordinate of rectangle

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: Width of the rectangle

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: Height of the rectangle

parameters.name: Radius
parameters.description: Radius of rounded corners.

parameters.name: FillMode
parameters.description: Fill mode. Can be FillNoClose, StrokeNoClose, FillStrokeNoClose, Fill, Stroke, FillStroke, FillEvOdd, FillStrokeEvOdd, FillEvOddNoClose, FillStrokeEvOddNoClose, NoFill or Close.

functions.name: DynaPDF.RoundRectEx
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.RoundRectEx"; PDF; PosX; PosY; Width; Height; RadiusWidth; RadiusHeight; FillMode )
functions.description:
The function draws a rectangle with elliptical corners. The parameter RadiusWidth must not be greater than the half width of the rectangle and the parameter RadiusHeight must not be greater than the half height of the rectangle. If the coordinate system is bottom-up the point PosX, PosY defines the lower left corner of the rectangle. If the coordinate system is top-down it defines the upper left corner. The draw direction can be changed with the function DynaPDF.SetDrawDirection . A rectangle is a closed path that can be filled, stroked or both. It is also possible to draw a rectangle invisible to apply the filling rules nonzero winding number or even-odd. The filling rules are described under DynaPDF.ClipPath . The parameter FillMode is ignored if the rectangle is drawn inside a clipping path. The fill modes are described under DynaPDF.ClosePath . See also RoundRectEx function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Draw blue round rectangle width different x/y radii: Copy Text Copy XML Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetStrokeColor "; $pdf ; 0; 0; 0) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetFillColor "; $pdf ; 0; 0; ,8) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetLineWidth "; $pdf ; 5) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( "DynaPDF.RoundRectEx"; $pdf ; 100; 100; 300; 200; 30; 20; "fillStroke" ) ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SetStrokeColor"; $pdf; 0; 0; 0) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>

functions.name: DynaPDF.Save
functions.description: Saves the current PDF to a container value which you can assign to a container field. The plugin also renders a JPEG for preview and includes it (with DynaPDF Pro). If you created PDF to a file path and not in memory, this method just returns OK after closing the file. Before you call this function, you can call DynaPDF.OpenOutputFile or DynaPDF.OpenOutputFileEncrypted to open a destination file for the PDF. See Plugin.SetPreviewSize to control size of preview picture. If you have an open page, we close it automatically for you before doing the import.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: Optionally the file name to be associated with the container value. The file name should include pdf file extension.

parameters.name: Preview
parameters.description: Optional, whether to include a preview JPEG. Default is 1 for including one. Pass zero to disable preview.

functions.result: Returns container value with PDF file.

examples.label: Save PDF:
examples.text_code:
Set Field [
MyTable::ContainerField
;
MBS
( "DynaPDF.Save";
$PDF
; "report.pdf" ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.Save"; $PDF; "report.pdf" ) </Calculation>
    <Field table="MyTable" id="99999" name="ContainerField"/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    <Field table="MyTable" id="99999" name="ContainerField"/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFile
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFile"; PDF; CertFilePath { ; Password; Reason; Location; FileName; Preview } )
functions.description:
Saves and signs the current PDF. On success you either get back the PDF as container value (in memory PDF) or it returns OK after writing PDF to file (file based PDF). The plugin also renders a JPEG for preview and includes it within the container value (with DynaPDF Pro). On Windows you can use WinCrypt with DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileWinCrypt function and optionally use dialog to pick the certificate with DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileWinCryptDialog from the certificate store. For longer keys with 2048 and 4096 bits, please check DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt function. See Plugin.SetPreviewSize to control size of preview picture. If you have an open page, we close it automatically for you before doing the import. See also SaveAndSignFile function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Save PDF: Copy Text Copy XML Set Field [ MyTable::ContainerField ; MBS ( "DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFile"; $PDF ; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.pfx"; $password ; "Test"; ""; "report.pdf" ) ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFile"; $PDF; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.pfx"; $password; "Test"; ""; "report.pdf" ) </Calculation>
    <Field table="MyTable" id="99999" name="ContainerField"/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> See also DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.PlaceSigFieldValidateIcon DynaPDF.Save DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt1 DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt2 DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileWinCrypt DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileWinCryptDialog PKCS12.SignData Plugin.SetPreviewSize Release notes Version 14.2 Added CertificateName parameter to DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileWinCryptDialog function to skip dialog and pick certificate by name. Version 13.4 Added new parameter useDetached for DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt1 function. Version 13.1 Added DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt1 and DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt2 functions to sign a PDF using external PKCS7 signature provider. Version 11.5 Fixed documentation for DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt and added missing ContactInfo parameter. Version 11.1 Fixed bug in DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt function. Version 11.0 Added DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt function to sign PDF file with 2048 or 4096 bit key length. Version 10.1 Added DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileWinCrypt and DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileWinCryptDialog functions. Example Databases DynaPDF/Merge PDFs DynaPDF/Signature Appearance Blog Entries Unlocking the Power of PDFs in FileMaker with DynaPDF and the MBS FileMaker Plugin Combined Components with MBS FileMaker Plugin New in MBS FileMaker Plugin 11.0 News about the MBS FileMaker Plugin 10.2 Digitally signing PDF with DynaPDF and custom signature appearance MBS Filemaker Plugin, version 3.3pr7 This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 25th September 2024 DynaPDF.Save - DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: CertFilePath
parameters.description: File path to a PKCS#12 certificate file.

parameters.name: Password
parameters.description: Password to decrypt the cert's private key.

parameters.name: Reason
parameters.description: Reason string.

parameters.name: Location
parameters.description: Signers location string

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name to be associated with the container value. The file name should include pdf file extension.

parameters.name: Preview
parameters.description: Whether to include a preview JPEG. Default is 1 for including one. Pass zero to disable preview.

functions.result: Returns PDF container, OK or error.

examples.xml_code:

    <Field table="MyTable" id="99999" name="ContainerField"/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt"; PDF; PKCS12 Reference { ; ContactInfo; Location; Reason; Signer; FileName; Preview } )
functions.description:
Saves and signs the current PDF. On success you either get back the PDF as container value (in memory PDF) or it returns OK after writing PDF to file (file based PDF). The plugin also renders a JPEG for preview and includes it within the container value (with DynaPDF Pro). Same as DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFile , but the signing is done by MBS plugin itself instead of DynaPDF. You pass the PKCS#12 key and certificate by reference number.  This way we can support more keys than DynaPDF directly, e.g. with 2048 or 4096 bits. See Plugin.SetPreviewSize to control size of preview picture. If you have an open page, we close it automatically for you before doing the import. See also SaveAndSignFileExt function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Sign a PDF with PKCS#12 key: Copy Text Copy XML # Initialize DynaPDF if needed If [ MBS (" DynaPDF.IsInitialized ")  ≠  1 ] Perform Script [ Specified: From list ; “InitDynaPDF” ; Parameter:    ] End If # New PDF Set Variable [ $pdf ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.New ") ] # # output to file Set Variable [ $destPath ; Value: MBS (" Folders.UserDesktop ") & "/test.pdf" ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.OpenOutputFile "; $pdf ; $destPath ) ] # # Load PDF from container Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer "; $pdf ; Merge PDFs::InputPDF ) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile "; $pdf ; 1) ] # # or sign it on saving Set Variable [ $pkey ; Value: MBS ( "PKCS12.ReadFromFile"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test_cert.pfx"; "123456" ) ] Set Field [ Merge PDFs::MergeStatus ; MBS ("DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt"; $pdf ; $pkey ; "Hello"; "World"; ""; ""; "Merged.pdf") ] # # cleanup Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( "PKCS12.Release"; $pkey ) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Release "; $pdf ) ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Initialize DynaPDF if needed</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("DynaPDF.IsInitialized")  ≠  1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="1" name="Perform Script">
    <Calculation>  </Calculation>
    <Script id="99999" name="InitDynaPDF"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> New PDF</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.New") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pdf</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> output to file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Folders.UserDesktop") & "/test.pdf" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$destPath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.OpenOutputFile"; $pdf; $destPath) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Load PDF from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer"; $pdf; Merge PDFs::InputPDF) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile"; $pdf; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> or sign it on saving</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PKCS12.ReadFromFile"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test_cert.pfx"; "123456" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pkey</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt"; $pdf; $pkey; "Hello"; "World"; ""; ""; "Merged.pdf") </Calculation>
    <Field table="Merge PDFs" id="99999" name="MergeStatus "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PKCS12.Release"; $pkey) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.Release"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> See also DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.OpenOutputFile DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer DynaPDF.Release DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt1 DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt2 Folders.UserDesktop PKCS12.ReadFromContainer PKCS12.ReadFromFile Plugin.SetPreviewSize Release notes Version 13.4 Added new parameter useDetached for DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt1 function. Version 13.1 Added DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt1 and DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt2 functions to sign a PDF using external PKCS7 signature provider. Version 11.5 Fixed documentation for DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt and added missing ContactInfo parameter. Version 11.1 Fixed bug in DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt function. Version 11.0 Added DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt function to sign PDF file with 2048 or 4096 bit key length. examples.text_code:
# Initialize DynaPDF if needed
If [
MBS
("
DynaPDF.IsInitialized
")  ≠  1 ]
Perform Script [ Specified: From list ; “InitDynaPDF” ; Parameter:    ]
End If
# New PDF
Set Variable [
$pdf
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.New
") ]
#
# output to file
Set Variable [
$destPath
; Value:
MBS
("
Folders.UserDesktop
") & "/test.pdf" ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.OpenOutputFile
";
$pdf
;
$destPath
) ]
#

; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.OpenPDFFromContainer
";
$pdf
;
Merge PDFs::InputPDF
) ]
Set Variable [

; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.ImportPDFFile
";
$pdf
; 1) ]
#

; Value:

Set Field [

$pdf
;
$pkey
; "Hello"; "World"; ""; ""; "Merged.pdf") ]
#

functions.name: DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt1
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt1"; PDF { ; ContactInfo; Location; Reason; Signer; SignatureSize; useDetached } )
functions.description: Saves and signs the current PDF. On success you either get back the PDF as container value (in memory PDF) or it returns OK after writing PDF to file (file based PDF). No preview picture is added. Please use DynaPDF.GeneratePreview function if this is needed. Same as DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt , you need to create the signature and then call DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt2 function to pass the final signature as hex string. Returns hex encoded hash to sign. As of v13.1 we have no MBS function to perform such a signature, but.we may add that if needed. But this function was made for people using external hardware to perform the signature. If you have an open page, we close it automatically for you before doing the import.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: ContactInfo
parameters.description: The contact info.

parameters.name: Location
parameters.description: Signers location string

parameters.name: Reason
parameters.description: Reason string.

parameters.name: Signer
parameters.description: Signers name string

parameters.name: SignatureSize
parameters.description: The byte size for the signature expected. This is an estimate on how much space to reserve in the PDF. Default is 2048 bytes, enough for a 1024 bit signature. You may sign a dummy text to check how big the signature is before signing the PDF.

parameters.name: useDetached
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return data to sign instead of SHA1 hash of data to sign. Be aware that the data to sign with detached mode can be several megabytes big depending on the PDF size.

examples.label: Start signing process:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$hash
; Value:
MBS
("DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt1";
$pdf
; "Test User"; "Chicago"; "") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt1"; $pdf; "Test User"; "Chicago"; "") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$hash</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Manually sign PDF content:
examples.text_code:
# manually load certificate and key
Set Variable [
$pkcs12
; Value:
MBS
( "PKCS12.ReadFromFile"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test_cert.pfx"; "123456") ]
# sign a test string to know size in advance
Set Variable [
$SampleSignature
; Value:
MBS
( "PKCS12.SignData";
$pkcs12
; "hex"; "C9097E23869BC9903CE89E9562230C6FDCBC0F06"; ""; "hex"; "" ) ]
Set Variable [
$estimatedSignatureSize
; Value: Length(
$SampleSignature
) / 2 + 5 ]
# now save and produce hash
Set Variable [
$hashToSign
; Value:
MBS
("DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt1";
$pdf
; "My ContactInfo"; "My Location"; "My Reason"; "My Signer";
$estimatedSignatureSize
) ]
# sign the hash
Set Variable [
$Signature
; Value:
MBS
( "PKCS12.SignData";
$pkcs12
; "hex";
$HashToSign
; ""; "hex"; "" ) ]
# and finish the PDF
Set Field [
Merge PDFs::MergeStatus
;
MBS
("
DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt2
";
$pdf
;
$signature
; "Merged.pdf") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> manually load certificate and key</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PKCS12.ReadFromFile"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test_cert.pfx"; "123456") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pkcs12</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> sign a test string to know size in advance</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PKCS12.SignData"; $pkcs12; "hex"; "C9097E23869BC9903CE89E9562230C6FDCBC0F06"; ""; "hex"; "" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$SampleSignature</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Length($SampleSignature) / 2 + 5 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$estimatedSignatureSize</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now save and produce hash</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt1"; $pdf; "My ContactInfo"; "My Location"; "My Reason"; "My Signer"; $estimatedSignatureSize) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$hashToSign</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> sign the hash</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PKCS12.SignData"; $pkcs12; "hex"; $HashToSign; ""; "hex"; "" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Signature</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and finish the PDF</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileExt2"; $pdf; $signature; "Merged.pdf") </Calculation>
    <Field table="Merge PDFs" id="99999" name="MergeStatus "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileWinCrypt
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileWinCrypt"; PDF; SignatureContainer { ; Password; ContactInfo; Location; Reason; FileName; Preview; DetachedSignature } )
functions.description: Saves and signs the current PDF with dialog to pick certificate. On success you either get back the PDF as container value (in memory PDF) or it returns OK after writing PDF to file (file based PDF). The plugin also renders a JPEG for preview and includes it within the container value (with DynaPDF Pro). On Windows you can use WinCrypt with DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileWinCrypt function and optionally use dialog to pick the certificate with DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileWinCryptDialog from the certificate store. Use DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFile for cross platform signing. See Plugin.SetPreviewSize to control size of preview picture. If you have an open page, we close it automatically for you before doing the import.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: SignatureContainer
parameters.description: The container with the PKCS#12 file.

parameters.name: Password
parameters.description: The password to read the signature. Leave empty if not encrypted.

parameters.name: ContactInfo
parameters.description: Optional contact info.

parameters.name: Location
parameters.description: Optional signers location string

parameters.name: Reason
parameters.description: Optional reason string.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: Optionally the file name to be associated with the container value. The file name should include pdf file extension.

parameters.name: Preview
parameters.description: Optional, whether to include a preview JPEG. Default is 1 for including one. Pass zero to disable preview.

parameters.name: DetachedSignature
parameters.description: Whether to use a detached signature. Pass 1 for detached signature vs. 0 for SHA1 signature. Default is 0.

functions.name: DynaPDF.SaveGraphicState
functions.description: The function saves the current graphics state. A saved graphics state must be restored before the page, template or pattern will be closed. see also DynaPDF.RestoreGraphicState . You can use DynaPDF.SaveGraphicState with RestorePageCoords option to define whether this saves the page coordinate option (bottom-up or top-down). See also SaveGraphicState function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.ClipPath DynaPDF.CreateRadialShading DynaPDF.DrawCircle DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.Rectangle DynaPDF.Save DynaPDF.ScaleCoords DynaPDF.SetAlpha DynaPDF.SkewCoords DynaPDF.TranslateCoords Example Databases Barcode/DynaPDF Barcode DynaPDF/Coordinates DynaPDF/Create Text as PDFA DynaPDF/Page Layouting DynaPDF/Place Template DynaPDF/Signature Appearance DynaPDF/Watermark pages Blog Entries Page Layouting in DynaPDF DynaPDF graphics flags This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 2nd August 2023 DynaPDF.SaveAndSignFileWinCryptDialog - DynaPDF.ScaleCoords 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

functions.name: DynaPDF.ScaleCoords
functions.description:
The function scales the coordinate system by applying a transformation matrix. It is highly recommended to save the graphics state beforehand, otherwise it is very difficult or impossible to restore the coordinate system later. After scaling the coordinate system, bottom-up coordinates are active. It is not possible to use top- down coordinates inside a scaled coordinate system. Please do not try to scale/skew/translate back later. use DynaPDF.SaveGraphicState and DynaPDF.RestoreGraphicState . See also ScaleCoords function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Scale and draw rectangle: Copy Text Copy XML MBS ( " DynaPDF.SaveGraphicState "; $PDF ) MBS ( "DynaPDF.ScaleCoords"; $PDF ; 2; 2) MBS ( " DynaPDF.Rectangle "; $PDF ; 0; 0; 200; 100; "stroke" ) MBS ( " DynaPDF.RestoreGraphicState "; $PDF ) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.SaveGraphicState"; $PDF )
MBS( "DynaPDF.ScaleCoords"; $PDF; 2; 2)
MBS( "DynaPDF.Rectangle"; $PDF; 0; 0; 200; 100; "stroke" )
MBS( "DynaPDF.RestoreGraphicState"; $PDF )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> See also DynaPDF.GetPageCoords DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.Rectangle DynaPDF.RestoreGraphicState DynaPDF.RotateCoords DynaPDF.Save DynaPDF.SaveGraphicState DynaPDF.SetPageCoords DynaPDF.SkewCoords Example Databases DynaPDF/Coordinates This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 DynaPDF.SaveGraphicState - DynaPDF.SetActiveSigField 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: Scale factor of the x-axis

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: Scale factor of the y-axis

functions.result: Returns OK on success and error on failure.

functions.name: DynaPDF.SetActiveSigField
functions.description: Sets the signature field that should be signed. If a PDF file contains more than one signature field, the first signature field without a value is selected for signing by default. If a different signature field should be signed, call this function with the desired handle. If the signature field contains already a value or signature, the function returns with an error and the field to be signed is not changed. See also SetActiveSigField function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.New Release notes Version 15.3 Added DynaPDF.SetActiveSigField function. functions.name: DynaPDF.SetAltFonts
functions.description: Fills an alternate font list created by DynaPDF.CreateAltFontList with data. Alternate font list are tested if one more glyphs were not found in the current font. Since the fonts in the list are used as fallback they should be compatible to the active font in regard to properties like serif, sans serif, proportional or fixed pitch, and so on. A list should be sorted by priority and not by name. That means index 0 should contain a font name that is most probably the best match. If this one was not available or incompatible then the next font in the list will be tested until the end of the list was reached. If no suitable font was found then system fonts are tested. The fonts in the list should support the language that must be output. Otherwise, the fonts can never be selected. An alternate font list can be arbitrary large but it is usually best to restrict the number fonts in a list to speed up processing. A list can contain font names which are not available on the system. This causes no error. Non existing fonts are simply ignored. This makes it possible to define suitable alternate font lists statically. Such lists can then be loaded on demand. After a list was filled with data it can be activated with DynaPDF.ActivateAltFontList . If a list is no longer needed delete it with DynaPDF.DeleteAltFontList . Please note that alternate font lists are used only, if complex text layout was enabled. This can be done by setting the flag ComplexText with DynaPDF.SetGStateFlags . See also SetAltFonts function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.ActivateAltFontList DynaPDF.CreateAltFontList DynaPDF.DeleteAltFontList DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.SetFieldFont DynaPDF.SetFont DynaPDF.SetFontEx DynaPDF.SetGStateFlags DynaPDF.SetUseStdFonts Release notes Version 12.0 Added DynaPDF.ActivateAltFontList , DynaPDF.CreateAltFontList , DynaPDF.DeleteAltFontList and DynaPDF.SetAltFonts functions. functions.name: DynaPDF.SetAnnotBorderStyle
functions.description: Sets or changes the border style of an annotation. Note that the border style has no effect if the border width is set to zero or if the border color is set to NO_COLOR (=4294967281) (see DynaPDF.SetAnnotColor , DynaPDF.SetAnnotBorderWidth ). Note also that not all styles are meaningful for all annotation types. For example, while link annotations support all border styles, FreeText annotations support the styles Solid and Dashed only. File Aattach, Sound, Stamp, or Text annotations for example support no border style because these annotation types contain no appearance on which a border style could be applied. Setting the border style to an unsupported value causes no error; the value will be ignored instead. When changing the border style of a Free Text annotation DynaPDF must rebuild the appearance stream of the annotation. The text position can slightly change especially if the border width was changed too. See also DynaPDF.FreeTextAnnot . See also SetAnnotBorderStyle function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.FreeTextAnnot DynaPDF.GetBorderStyle DynaPDF.GetFieldBorderStyle DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.SetAnnotBorderWidth DynaPDF.SetAnnotColor DynaPDF.SetAnnotLineEndStyle DynaPDF.SetAnnotOpenState DynaPDF.SetBorderStyle DynaPDF.SetFieldBorderStyle This function checks for a license. Created 25th November 2014 , last changed 25th November 2014 DynaPDF.SetAltFonts - DynaPDF.SetAnnotBorderWidth 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: DynaPDF.SetAnnotBorderWidth
functions.description: Sets or changes the border width of an annotation. Although the line width can be set to any positive floating point number, it should be set to full integer values between 0 through 12. If no border should be drawn set the line width to zero. When changing the border width of a Free Text annotation DynaPDF must rebuild the appearance stream of the annotation. The text position depends on the line width of the border. Changing the border width changes also the position of the text. See also DynaPDF.FreeTextAnnot . See also SetAnnotBorderWidth function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.FreeTextAnnot DynaPDF.GetFieldBorderWidth DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.SetAnnotBorderStyle DynaPDF.SetFieldBorderWidth DynaPDF.Table.SetBorderWidth This function checks for a license. functions.name: DynaPDF.SetAnnotColor
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.SetAnnotColor"; PDF; AnnotationHandle; ColorSelector; ColorSpace; ColorValue )
functions.description: Sets or changes the color of an annotation. The color can be defined in any device color space. However, at time of publication the function converts the color back to DeviceRGB. Note that not all annotation types support a background or border color. For example, link annotations support a border color but no background or text color. When changing a color of a Free Text annotation DynaPDF must rebuild the appearance stream of the annotation. This can cause slightly changes in the text position. See also DynaPDF.FreeTextAnnot . See also SetAnnotColor function in DynaPDF manual.
