functions.name: DynaPDF.WriteStyledText
functions.description:
Writes styled text on current page. Converts for you the styled text to the formatted text commands from DynaPDF. Use DynaPDF.GetFTextHeightEx or DynaPDF.GetFTextHeight to calculate the space needed for the text if you want to create a matching text rectangle for vertical alignment. See DynaPDF.AllowPageBreak for whether to do page breaks or not. Please set a font first with DynaPDF.SetFont function before you write on the page. As this function uses font settings from FileMaker field, it may ignore whatever font or style you loaded before. Internally calls DynaPDF.ConvertStyledText and DynaPDF.WriteFText . MBS Plugin in version 11.5 with DynaPDF 4.0.62.160 adds support for double underlined text. Warning: FileMaker does not pass style information from the layout used to give a field as default style. If you put text into a field and use the style of the field, then the plugin doesn't know this detail. You have to explicit use different font for FileMaker to pass it. e.g. if your field is set to Verdana 12 and you have "Hello World" inside with Hello using Verdana 12 and World using Times 13, then the plugin would get passed "Hello" without font, since it matches the default for the field. See also WriteStyledText function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Write some styled text on a PDF page: Copy Text Copy XML Set Variable [ $pdf ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.New ")] #Add page Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.AppendPage "; $pdf )] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetFont "; $pdf ; "Helvetica"; 0; 12)] #Write some text Set Variable [ $pw ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.GetPageWidth "; $pdf )] Set Variable [ $ph ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.GetPageHeight "; $pdf )] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetFillColor "; $pdf ; 0; 0; 0)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetTextRect "; $pdf ; 100; $ph -100; $pw -200; $ph -200)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ("DynaPDF.WriteStyledText"; $pdf ; "left"; Create Text::Text )] #End page Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.EndPage "; $pdf )] #Render one page as Picture Set Variable [ $PDFData ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Save "; $pdf ; "hello.pdf")] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.Release "; $pdf )] #Put in Container Set Field [ Create Text::PDF ; $PDFData ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.New")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pdf</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Add page</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.AppendPage"; $pdf)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SetFont"; $pdf; "Helvetica"; 0; 12)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Write some text</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.GetPageWidth"; $pdf)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pw</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.GetPageHeight"; $pdf)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ph</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SetFillColor"; $pdf; 0; 0; 0)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SetTextRect"; $pdf; 100; $ph-100; $pw-200; $ph-200)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.WriteStyledText"; $pdf; "left"; Create Text::Text)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>End page</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.EndPage"; $pdf)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Render one page as Picture</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.Save"; $pdf; "hello.pdf")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$PDFData</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.Release"; $pdf)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Put in Container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$PDFData</Calculation>
    <Field table="Create Text" id="99999" name="PDF"/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> See also DynaPDF.ConvertStyledText DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.Release DynaPDF.Save DynaPDF.SetPageBreakExpression DynaPDF.WriteFText DynaPDF.WriteFTextEx DynaPDF.WriteFTextExRotated DynaPDF.WriteStyledTextEx DynaPDF.WriteStyledTextExRotated Release notes Version 14.1 Improved font mapping for DynaPDF.WriteStyledText to better handle bold fonts. Version 13.2 Added new highlight style for DynaPDF.ConvertStyledText , DynaPDF.WriteStyledText and DynaPDF.WriteStyledTextEx . Version 11.0 Added leading factor parameter for DynaPDF.ConvertStyledText , DynaPDF.WriteStyledText and DynaPDF.WriteStyledTextEx . Version 7.4 Added DynaPDF.WriteFTextExRotated and DynaPDF.WriteStyledTextExRotated . Writes styled text on current page. Converts for you the styled text to the formatted text commands from DynaPDF. Use DynaPDF.GetFTextHeightEx or DynaPDF.GetFTextHeight to calculate the space needed for the text if you want to create a matching text rectangle for vertical alignment. parameters.description: If you pass a value > 0, the plugin will add \LD[] commands to styled text to set leading relative to font size. This way you define the spacing beteween lines.

examples.label: Write styled text with rectangle:
examples.text_code:
# page size
Set Variable [
$pw
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.GetPageWidth
";
$pdf
) ]
Set Variable [
$ph
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.GetPageHeight
";
$pdf
) ]
# set black color
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
DynaPDF.SetFillColor
";
$pdf
; 0; 0; 0) ]
# write styled text

$pdf

$ph

examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> page size</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.GetPageWidth"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pw</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.GetPageHeight"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ph</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set black color</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SetFillColor"; $pdf; 0; 0; 0) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> write styled text</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.WriteStyledTextEx"; $pdf; 100; $ph-100; $pw-200; $ph-200; "left"; Create Text::Text) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DynaPDF.WriteStyledTextExRotated
functions.signature: MBS( "DynaPDF.WriteStyledTextExRotated"; PDF; PosX; PosY; Width; Height; Align; Text; Alpha )
functions.description: Writes styled text on current page. Same as DynaPDF.WriteStyledTextEx , but with additional rotation in center of box. See also WriteStyledTextExRotated function in DynaPDF manual. See also DynaPDF.New DynaPDF.WriteFTextExRotated DynaPDF.WriteStyledText DynaPDF.WriteStyledTextEx Release notes Version 7.4 Added DynaPDF.WriteFTextExRotated and DynaPDF.WriteStyledTextExRotated . Blog Entries MBS FileMaker Plugin, version 7.4pr2 FileMaker Magazin Ausgabe 5/2021, Seite 28 This function checks for a license. Created 10th August 2017 , last changed 10th August 2017 DynaPDF.WriteStyledTextEx - DynaPDF.WriteText 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference returned from DynaPDF.New .

parameters.name: PosX
parameters.description: X-Coordinate of output rectangle

parameters.name: PosY
parameters.description: Y-Coordinate of output rectangle

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: Width of output rectangle

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: Height of output rectangle or -1.

parameters.name: Align
parameters.description: The text alignment. Can be left, center, right or justify.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to write.

parameters.name: Alpha
parameters.description: The rotation angle in degrees. From 0 to 360 degrees.

functions.name: DynaPDF.WriteText
functions.description:
Writes text on current page. Please set a font first with DynaPDF.SetFont function before you write on the page. See also WriteText function in DynaPDF manual. Examples Write something: Copy Text Copy XML MBS ("DynaPDF.WriteText"; $pdf ; 111; 323; Invoice::Invoice ID) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.WriteText"; $pdf; 111; 323; Invoice::Invoice ID)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Draw text over rectangle: Copy Text Copy XML # where? Set Variable [ $x ; Value: 100 ] Set Variable [ $y ; Value: 100 ] # the text Set Variable [ $text ; Value: "Some blue text" ] # set the font before measuring Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetFont "; $pdf ; "Helvetica"; 0; 12) ] # measure now Set Variable [ $width ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.GetTextWidth "; $pdf ; $text ) ] # draw rectangle width some addition 5 point margin Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetFillColor "; $pdf ; ,8; ,8; ,8) // light gray ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( " DynaPDF.Rectangle "; $pdf ; $x - 5; $y - 5 - 2; $width + 10; 5 + 12 + 5; "fill") ] # and draw text on top Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetFillColor "; $pdf ; 0; 0; 1) // 100% blue ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ("DynaPDF.WriteText"; $pdf ; 100; $x ; $text ) ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> where?</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>100 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$x</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>100 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$y</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> the text</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"Some blue text" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set the font before measuring</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SetFont"; $pdf; "Helvetica"; 0; 12) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> measure now</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.GetTextWidth"; $pdf; $text ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$width</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> draw rectangle width some addition 5 point margin</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SetFillColor"; $pdf; ,8; ,8; ,8) // light gray </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DynaPDF.Rectangle"; $pdf; $x - 5; $y - 5 - 2; $width + 10; 5 + 12 + 5; "fill") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and draw text on top</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.SetFillColor"; $pdf; 0; 0; 1) // 100% blue </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DynaPDF.WriteText"; $pdf; 100; $x; $text) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet> Write checkmark: Copy Text Copy XML Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" DynaPDF.SetFont "; $pdf ; "Zapf Dingbats"; 0; 12) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ("DynaPDF.WriteText"; $pdf ; 100; $x ; Char(10004)) // "✔︎" ] <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

    </Repetition>
